result.position: No hard spec-size number. Adopt machine-checkable regularity invariants (one spelling per construct; zero context-dependent rules; every rule universally quantified with an empty exception list; each fact stated exactly once; bounded-and-ratcheted rule count per layer) enforced on the spec as a checked artifact, and validate spec compactness with measured in-context learning curves. Any numeric budget must be measurement-derived after model data exists — never pre-registered before it.
result.coreArgument:
1) A hard token cap operationalizes the wrong reading of D2. The owner's corrected directive names PROPERTIES, not a number: "few rules, uniform, no special cases, each stated once." Three of those four are directly checkable structural invariants; none is a token count. What D2 says eats the budget is IRREGULARITY — sugar, alternate spellings, inference, context-dependent elision, special cases. Regularity invariants target exactly that enumerated list; a token cap targets a proxy for it. Adopting the invariants is operationalizing the directive; adopting a number is legislating beyond it on zero evidence.

2) Any specific number is arbitrary today by the corpus's own discipline. There are ZERO evidence cards on in-context language acquisition (the round-2 syntax verdict records that even the null-hypothesis win on syntax was procedural, not empirical, and orders a new syntax/AI-codegen evidence lane precisely because "every behavioral claim in this debate is currently uncardable"). caveats.md's standing rule requires exact card citation for design decisions, and decision-gates.md forbids a feature becoming law "merely because it is optimizer-attractive" — the same bar must apply to a numeric target that is merely governance-attractive. Choosing 20K vs 50K vs 100K tokens today has no card behind it; whichever number is picked will silently encode an untested model-behavior claim ("performance degrades above X") as law. The project's established pattern is the opposite: run the ordered measurement program, pre-commit thresholds, then decide (round-2 synthesis empirical program; round-2 syntax's gated A/B for the infix codec). The right analogue here is a learning-curve gate, not a pre-registered constant.

3) Token counts measure the spec's PROSE, not the language's DESIGN. Two specs with identical rule content can differ ~2x in tokens through editing style, formatting, notation density, and example count; token count also varies by tokenizer and model. The corpus already burned itself on exactly this class of proxy: round-2 syntax's 1.35–1.48x token figure was flagged as a regex proxy on fragments, explicitly insufficient for decisions until re-run with real tokenizers on whole programs. A hard cap would enshrine an even weaker proxy — spec-prose tokens as a stand-in for learnability — as a permanent design constraint. Regularity invariants, by contrast, are invariant under prose style: an alternate spelling, a context-dependent rule, or an exception list is detectable in the spec's formal content regardless of how it is worded.

4) A hard cap Goodharts against D2's actual goal. Under a binding cap, the cheapest tokens to cut are exactly the ones D2 values: worked examples (D2 says the language is taught via "spec + examples"), fully spelled-out universal quantifiers, and redundancy-free but explicit statements of each rule. The predictable compression moves — merging rules into denser context-dependent formulations, terser notation, fewer examples — REDUCE tokens while INCREASING irregularity and model guessing surface. This mirrors the round-2 elision finding at the spec level: elision of explicit statements doesn't make the information free, it converts it into unverified inference the model must perform — the exact error surface D2 bans. A cap optimizes description length; D2's goal is acquisition reliability. When proxy and goal diverge under pressure, law should bind the goal.

5) A cap creates false scarcity fights it cannot legitimately adjudicate. D1 is non-negotiable and the round-3 envelope (proven-else-checked, obligation ledger, FFI declaration frames) has whatever rule count soundness requires; the round-2 floors are law. If the cap binds against safety-envelope or floor rules, it must yield — so against the fixed constraints it is either a dead rule (never binds) or a wrongly-binding one (pressures soundness machinery). Every added spec rule IS a real cost — but the carded form of that cost is F003's lesson that facts are sound only if frontend obligations are true and maintained: rule count per layer is a soundness-surface budget, which is exactly what the ratcheted rule-count invariant prices, without a token constant.

6) What regularity invariants force architecturally — more, not less, than a cap forces. (a) THE SPEC BECOMES A CHECKED ARTIFACT: the grammar/typing/annotation-vocabulary spec is stored in a machine-checkable form on which the toolchain enumerates rules per layer, detects alternate productions reaching the same core node (one-spelling check), detects side conditions referencing surrounding context (zero context-dependence check), verifies every rule's exception set is empty, and content-addresses rule statements so each fact is stated once — the same consistency-layer architecture (round-2 synthesis mechanisms 1–4) the language already applies to programs, now applied reflexively to the spec. N006 is the carded precedent that single-canonical-form discipline is a workable, operationalized engineering practice, and F006 is the carded precedent that restriction/uniformity is the verification-first route. (b) A SPEC-REGRESSION RATCHET enters decision-gates.md: every proposal must declare its delta (+N rules, 0 new spellings, 0 exceptions, 0 context-dependencies) and rule count per layer may not grow without passing a gate — "few" is honestly operationalized as a monotone ratchet that binds immediately, rather than an arbitrary constant. (c) COMPOSITION PRESSURE: because new rules are gated, new capabilities must compose from existing rules — e.g., contract-edit gating already applies one mechanism identically to signatures, law bundles, and lifetime contracts; the invariant regime generalizes that as the default design move, which is precisely the uniformity D2 wants, forced structurally rather than by token accounting. (d) LEARNING-CURVE VALIDATION as the promotion requirement: in-context acquisition experiments (first-parse success, checker-error rate, repair iterations vs spec size and regularity) become the gate for spec growth and, later, the source of any measurement-derived budget — including the one genuinely hard outer bound, the deployment context window, which is a per-model-generation measured constraint, not a design-time constant.

7) The forcing-function argument for a cap is real but already satisfied. This project's cut discipline does not come from budgets — it comes from the null-hypothesis-CUT procedure that produced the round-2 cut list and the evidence-gate regime. The marginal governance value of a number is low here; its distortion risk (points 4–5) is not.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: SPARK is the corpus's confirmed pattern that verification-first quality comes from RESTRICTING semantics (uniformity, ownership discipline) rather than from size targets — direct precedent for regularity-as-target: the invariant regime restricts spec form (no exceptions, no context-dependence) exactly as SPARK restricts language semantics.

InstCombine's target-independent canonicalization shows single-canonical-form discipline operationalized as working infrastructure — precedent that 'one spelling per construct' is a checkable engineering invariant, not an aspiration, supporting machine-checkable regularity invariants over unmeasurable token vibes.

LLVM fact channels are sound only if frontend obligations are true and survive lowering — this cards the REAL cost of each added spec rule as soundness surface to be maintained, grounding the bounded-and-ratcheted rule-count-per-layer invariant in a verified concern instead of an uncarded token concern.

The mono-only generics floor was kept via evidence gates and an ordered measurement program with pre-committed thresholds — the project's demonstrated decision procedure is measure-then-threshold, not pre-registered numeric fiat; the learning-curve gate follows that same procedure for spec size.

Records that the 1.35–1.48x token figure was a regex proxy on fragments, explicitly insufficient until re-run with real tokenizers on whole programs, and that every AI-writer behavioral claim is uncardable — the on-record warning against enshrining token-count proxies as design law, and the template (gated A/B experiment) for admitting changes only after measurement.

The empirical-research section institutionalizes 'ordered measurement programs with pre-committed thresholds' and its consistency layer (content-addressing, one-definition rules, elaboration diffing) is the exact architecture the regularity invariants apply reflexively to the spec artifact itself.

Standing rule: design proposals must cite exact cards and state what remains unproven; 'no final language design decisions should be made from this plan' — fixing a numeric spec budget with zero acquisition cards would violate the corpus's own evidence discipline.

'A feature cannot become a language default merely because it is optimizer-attractive; it must pass the relevant gates' — the same gate discipline extended to governance numbers: a spec-size constant must pass an evidence gate (learning curves) before becoming law, and the ratchet slots into the existing gate table as a promotion requirement.

D2's corrected text names properties — 'few rules, uniform, no special cases, each stated once' — and enumerates irregularity (sugar, alternate spellings, inference, elision, special cases) as what eats the budget; the regularity invariants are a one-to-one operationalization of that enumerated list, while a token number appears nowhere in the directive.

concessions: Without any number, 'compactness' risks unenforceable drift: a language of 500 perfectly uniform, once-stated rules passes every regularity invariant yet could overwhelm a context window. My mitigation is the ratcheted rule-count-per-layer bound plus the learning-curve gate — but I concede the ratchet's initial per-layer bounds involve judgment, and a pure invariant regime without the ratchet would be insufficient.

Hard caps are genuinely superior commitment devices: a number is cheap to adjudicate, hard to argue around, and forces real prioritization fights; regularity invariants require careful definitions (what counts as one rule; do table rows count; is a schema with N instantiations one rule or N) and those definitions can themselves be gamed — one mega-rule with a huge uniform table is the mirror-image Goodhart of my own position.

There IS a physical outer bound: spec + examples + program text + diagnostics must fit in a working context with headroom. I concede a binding size constraint exists in the deployment sense; my claim is only that it is a measured, per-model-generation deployment constraint, not a design-time constant to engrave now.

A measurement-derived budget LATER is fully compatible with my position: once learning-curve experiments exist, pre-committing a threshold from that data is exactly the project's established pattern, and I would support it. My opposition is specifically to a number chosen before any model data.

Decision latency is a real cost of my regime: every spec-growth proposal now requires a gate pass and possibly an experiment, where a cap gives an instant verdict. In an AI-driven project where debates are cheap this is acceptable, but it is not free.

If the owner intends a cap primarily as a cultural forcing function rather than a truth claim about model behavior, my position under-serves that intent, and the ratchet is the closest substitute I can offer.
risks: Slow-accretion bloat discovered late: regularity-perfect rules can accumulate until acquisition degrades, and the degradation is only visible when learning-curve experiments are re-run — which may be expensive and infrequent, so the failure is detected after the spec has hardened and dependent design decisions have stacked on the extra rules.

Metric gaming inside the invariants: rule-consolidation tricks (mega-rules, parameterized rule schemas, giant uniform tables) can satisfy 'few rules, no exceptions' while increasing effective description mass; the rule-individuation definitions become load-bearing and contestable, recreating the arbitrariness I criticize in token caps one level down.

The validation methodology is itself unvalidated: in-context learning curves measured on one model generation may not transfer to other models or scales, so a spec tuned to measured curves could be overfit to a model snapshot — whereas a crude token cap is at least model-independent (that model-independence is also exactly why it is arbitrary, but the risk is real).

Governance asymmetry: a hard cap fails loudly (over budget → visible fight), while invariant-plus-ratchet regimes fail quietly (each gate pass looks locally justified); without a hostile reviewer role attached to the ratchet, the gate could rubber-stamp growth.

Experiment cost concentration: my position makes the learning-curve harness a critical-path dependency for all spec-growth decisions; if that harness is delayed or underpowered, the project either stalls spec evolution or waives the gate, and a waived gate is worse than a crude cap.

If the deployment outer bound turns out to be near current spec size, my 'measure later' stance loses its slack argument and an immediate numeric constraint would have been the honest choice — this is checkable early and cheaply, and should be.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'Irregularity harms in-context acquisition more than raw spec length' (my load-bearing claim). Experiment: hold total spec tokens constant; compare a uniform N-rule spec vs an (N−k)-rule spec plus k exceptions/context-dependent cases; measure first-parse success, checker-error rate, silent-semantic-error rate via differential testing, and repair iterations to green across multiple models.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'Spec token count above some threshold X degrades model acquisition' (the pro-cap side's load-bearing claim — equally uncarded). Experiment: learning curves vs spec length at fixed regularity — pad/expand a fixed rule set with prose verbosity and added examples; measure the same acquisition metrics as a function of tokens to locate any real degradation knee before ANY number is adopted.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'Examples and rules trade off at a measurable exchange rate for acquisition' (D2 teaches via spec + examples; a cap forces this tradeoff blind). Experiment: fixed total budget split across rules:examples at several ratios; measure acquisition metrics per ratio.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'Eliding spec statements increases model guessing errors' (my spec-level analogue of the round-2 elision finding). Experiment: minimal-spec vs fully-explicit-spec variants on constructs with plausible defaults; measure the rate at which models silently assume the wrong default and whether the checker catches it.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'In-context learning curves generalize across models and scales' (the validity premise of my own validation gate). Experiment: replicate the full acquisition battery across at least three model families and two scales per family; report cross-model rank stability of spec variants.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'Rule count per layer predicts model error better than total tokens' (justifies the ratchet's choice of controlled variable). Experiment: regression of acquisition metrics across a matrix of spec variants varying rule count and token count independently.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: 'A hard cap induces the predicted compression pathologies (rule merging, example deletion, denser context-dependent formulations)' (my Goodhart claim about design behavior under a cap — a claim about the DESIGN LOOP, itself untested). Experiment: run capped vs invariant-constrained spec-revision exercises with AI writers under growth pressure; audit resulting specs for irregularity deltas.

result.position: Repair-loop-primary: the in-context spec ships only the generative kernel (canonical grammar, annotation vocabulary, judgment forms, a worked example set, and a one-line index of rule families); the deterministic checker is the teacher. Every diagnostic is engineered as a spec citation — the violated rule's canonical stated-once text, a minimal node-path counterexample/witness, and a mechanical fix or edit-list — so the long tail of rules is taught lazily, exactly when violated. Load-bearing toolchain guarantees: citation-grade deterministic diagnostics, a rule-to-diagnostic coverage bijection audit, the round-2 edit-list machinery generalized, and a fast whole-program check loop where content-addressed memoized checking is infrastructure, not an optimization.
result.coreArgument: (1) LAZY TEACHING IS THE D2-OPTIMAL ALLOCATION OF A FIXED BUDGET. Corrected D2 (notes/user-directives.md) prices exactly one thing: rules stated up-front, in context, for every task. An eager spec must teach all rules for all programs; a diagnostic teaches exactly the rule THIS program violated, anchored to the exact node-path where it applies — perfect timing, perfect relevance, zero cost when not needed. For any single task the violated-rule set is a small subset of the full rule set, so moving the long tail of rule elaborations, interaction cases, and edge semantics from the spec to the checker's citation database converts a per-task fixed cost into a pay-on-violation cost. Crucially, this does NOT violate D2's stated-once discipline: the diagnostic QUOTES the rule's single canonical spec statement (rules are content-addressed; a citation is a spec fragment, not a second spelling of the rule). The spec stays the constitution; diagnostics are case law. (2) THE DECIDED ARCHITECTURE MAKES CHECKER-AS-TEACHER SOUND, WHICH IT IS NOT IN ORDINARY LANGUAGES. Lazy teaching fails wherever a rule can be violated without a diagnostic — unchecked conventions never generate lessons. The round-2 floors and round-3 spine eliminate that failure mode by construction: canonical-form-only with non-canonical input a hard error (round2-syntax), zero cross-boundary inference, zero implicit conversions (D010/N-series floor), closed world, exhaustive match with banned wildcards, checked ownership, verifier fact-provenance rule, and — after round 3 — no writer-emittable trusted assertion anywhere (round3-synthesis, cross-cutting invariant 1). Acceptance is therefore equivalent to spec-conformance for every spec-level rule: every violation produces exactly one lesson, and no rule is silently violable. The same floors make lessons STABLE: one spelling, deterministic local parse-error recovery, no heuristic cascades — the same defect always yields the same diagnostic bytes, so repair converges instead of thrashing. D009 (Julia) is the carded contrast: where non-conformance degrades silently instead of erroring, nothing teaches; our anti-inference/anti-elision floors keep every violation loud. (3) THE MARGINAL COST IS NEAR ZERO BECAUSE ROUND 2 ALREADY BUILT THE DELIVERY CHANNEL FOR CORRECTNESS REASONS. The synthesis already mandates, as decided obligations: machine-readable node-path diagnostics with cross-file provenance chains and template-level aggregation so one defect yields ONE repair site (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, 'Additions required' first bullet); site-enumerated mechanical edit lists on every closed-set change (same section); edit-list emission in the dispatch replacement and triple-artifact-naming diagnostics in the closures replacement ('Replacements' section); auto-derived error context and machine-readable trap reports with stack attribution (round2-error-handling, C002 the only carded post-violation observability precedent); and content-addressed caching of parse/typecheck/verification results on canonical bytes (round2-syntax 'what must be true': caching is the decided offset for canonical-form verbosity, riding the D001/D004 admissible compile-time channel). Repair-loop-primary adds only CONTENT to a channel that must exist anyway: the rule text, the minimal witness, the mechanical fix. The competing eager position pays scarce D2 budget to pre-teach rules most programs never touch AND still needs this entire diagnostic apparatus for correctness — it buys the same machinery and then duplicates its knowledge in context. (4) WHAT THE TOOLCHAIN MUST GUARANTEE (the position's obligations, stated as requirements): (i) Diagnostics-as-spec-citations: stable content-addressed rule IDs; every diagnostic carries the rule's canonical text, the node-path witness with the specific conflicting facts, and the mechanical fix/edit-list where one exists — the diagnostic format is part of the language deliverable, versioned with the spec. (ii) Rule-coverage bijection audit: every spec rule maps to at least one diagnostic emitter, checked mechanically when the toolchain itself builds; a rule without an emitter is unteachable and fails the toolchain build (the teaching-side analog of round-3's obligation-generation-completeness surface). (iii) Fast whole-program check loop: content-addressed function-level memoized checking and dev-mode codegen (round2-synthesis 'closed-world compile-loop pricing') with pre-committed latency thresholds; under repair-loop-primary this caching is load-bearing infrastructure — if the loop is slow, the teacher is slow, and the whole allocation inverts. (iv) Determinism and stability of diagnostic bytes and ordering; aggregation invariant (one defect, one repair site); no cascade errors. (v) Loop instrumentation as first-class output: iterations-to-green, repeat-violation rate per rule, diagnostics-per-iteration — this is the empirical dial that decides which rules migrate. (vi) A gated spec-evolution channel: rules violated on nearly every program (measured, not guessed) get distilled up into kernel-spec examples; distillation is a privileged operation under the round-3 unified gating regime so D2 regularity cannot erode by accretion. (5) HOW MUCH BURDEN MOVES: the constitution stays in context — the generative kernel a model needs to produce a plausible first draft at all — plus a rule-family index so the model knows what exists without knowing its details. Everything else moves to the loop. The exact split is frequency-driven and instrumented, not dogmatic: repair-loop-primary is the only position that BUILDS the measurement dial, so even if the initial split is wrong, it is self-correcting; the eager position has no comparable feedback mechanism and must guess the spec contents once, blind.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: SPARK is the carded precedent that a verification-first language restricts surface expressiveness so the checker embodies the rules — the precondition for checker-as-teacher: acceptance must coincide with spec-conformance, which our decided floors replicate.

Julia's silent performance degradation when type-stability conventions are violated is the carded counterexample to lazy teaching: unchecked conventions never generate lessons. It supports the claim that the anti-inference/anti-elision floors (hard errors, never silent fallback) are what make the repair loop a complete teacher here.

Monomorphization's compile-time/code-size tradeoff establishes compile time as an admissible, explicitly priced machine-writer cost channel — the channel repair-loop-primary spends on loop latency instead of spending D2 spec budget.

Codegen-units/incremental/LTO evidence shows the compile-time-vs-visibility tradeoff is real and manageable by caching granularity — the carded basis for content-addressed function-level memoized checking as the mechanism that keeps a whole-program check loop inside an interactive teaching budget.

Go's halt-on-detected-race is the corpus's only carded post-violation observability story; it anchors trap-with-report=abort as the dynamic half of the teacher (runtime violations also arrive as one machine-readable lesson with attribution).

LLVM noalias/alias.scope soundness is contingent on frontend-emitted facts being true — the frontend/checker is the pipeline's authoritative chokepoint, which is why its verdicts are trustworthy enough to serve as the teaching source of record.

The Alive2/InstCombine discipline — every rule application tested and verified — is the precedent for the obligation that diagnostic content itself needs proof-grade maintenance discipline: a mis-teaching diagnostic is a checker bug of the same severity class.

concessions: First-draft quality is bounded by the kernel spec alone: lazy teaching is reactive and cannot prevent a violation the model has not yet made in-session. Rules violated on virtually every program are cheaper taught eagerly; the position is repair-loop-PRIMARY, not repair-loop-only, and the frequency split must be measured, not asserted.

Each lesson costs a full generate/check/re-read iteration: K lazily-taught rules means at least K loop passes plus wasted generation tokens. If the rule-violation distribution is not heavy-tailed, the total-token economics can favor a larger eager spec — this is the honest arbitration point and it is currently unmeasured.

The teacher teaches soundness, not quality: diagnostics enforce spec rules, but D0's extreme-performance objective has no hard-error teacher. Lint-class channels (check-density, region-quality, reclamation-timeliness reports) exist in the decided architecture, but whether models act on non-error feedback is an open round-2 flag — a repair-loop-taught model may converge on sound-but-slow code the teacher never corrects.

Mechanical fixes bias toward local repair: edit-lists and per-site fixes can hill-climb a structurally wrong design into checker acceptance (the round-3 experiment battery's 'checked-op spam / rejection-pressure' concern). Architecture-level mistakes produce diagnostic storms whose correct fix is restructuring, and 'restructure-hint' diagnostics are undesigned.

Dynamic lessons under trap=abort arrive one per process death with loss of warm state — the loop-economics cost the error-handling verdict knowingly accepted; if the multi-process harness wall-clock measurement fails, the teaching loop inherits that failure.

Lessons wash out at context-window and session boundaries: repair-loop teaching is re-paid per session unless the distillation channel works, and that channel adds curation machinery the eager position does not need.

Closed-world whole-program check latency at AI-scale program sizes is round-2's biggest unpriced cost; this position is MORE exposed to it than any other, since the checker sits on the critical path of every lesson. If the content-addressed caching prototype misses its pre-committed thresholds, repair-loop-primary degrades to repair-loop-slow and the allocation argument inverts.
risks: Diagnostic text becomes TCB-adjacent: the checker was already trusted for soundness, but under this position its citation text is trusted for TEACHING — a wrong, stale, or misleading citation actively teaches a false rule and poisons the session, which is worse than a bare error. Diagnostics need N006-grade tested-and-versioned discipline, an unpriced engineering cost.

Rule-coverage gaps are silent: any spec rule without a diagnostic emitter is unteachable and invisible — the exact shape of round-3's obligation-generation-completeness surface (its largest unexamined one) reappearing on the teaching side. The bijection audit is the proposed control, but auditing 'every rule has an emitter' is itself a completeness claim with no validation precedent in the corpus.

Non-convergence: a model oscillating between two suggested fixes, or a diagnostic whose mechanical fix triggers a different diagnostic, produces livelock. Requires diagnostic stability, monotonicity design in the loop, and an oscillation detector in the harness — none of which is designed.

Checker-gaming equilibrium: a model taught exclusively by the checker optimizes for checker acceptance, not intent — conservative-frame spam, checked-op spam, and quota-pressure laundering are the round-3-named behaviors; the gated family and lint channels contain soundness but not quality erosion.

Spec-evolution channel as D2 erosion vector: distilling recurring diagnostics into the kernel spec, if not gated and curated, re-accretes exactly the irregular special-case mass D2 forbids — the gating regime must treat spec edits like contract edits.

Sunk-cost asymmetry is real but bounded: if diagnostics-as-citations underperform empirically, the fallback (grow the eager spec) is cheap to execute, but the rule database, coverage audit, and citation plumbing are sunk toolchain investment; conversely the eager position's sunk cost (a big spec) buys nothing if it also fails. The retreat path favors this position, but it is not free.
empiricalUntested: FLAG: 'Models repair violations faster and more reliably when diagnostics carry rule text + minimal counterexample + mechanical fix than with bare error messages.' Experiment: diagnostic-content ablation — identical kernel tasks, checker emitting (a) full spec-citation diagnostics, (b) rule-ID only, (c) bare location+message; measure iterations-to-green, within-session repeat-violation rate, and cross-rule generalization. Extends the round-2 'diagnostics repair-loop asymmetry' item.

FLAG: 'Kernel-only spec + repair loop reaches green in fewer total tokens (spec + generations + diagnostics + re-reads) than a full eager spec.' Experiment: spec-size ablation — kernel-only vs kernel+high-frequency-rules vs full spec on matched task sets; measure first-check success rate, loop count, wall-clock, and total tokens to green. This is THE arbitration experiment for this debate topic.

FLAG: 'Rule-violation frequency is heavy-tailed (few rules violated constantly, most rarely)' — the economic premise of lazy teaching. Experiment: rule-violation census over a generated-program corpus, per rule family, per model.

FLAG: 'A rule cited once in-session is not re-violated later in the same session' (lesson persistence). Experiment: repeat-violation decay curves within long sessions, including across context-window compaction events.

FLAG: 'Models execute site-enumerated edit lists as mechanical to-do lists with high fidelity' — inherited round-2 assumption underlying the dispatch-edit economics experiment; this position leans on it harder than any other.

FLAG: 'Models act effectively on lint-class (non-error) feedback in-loop' — existing round-2 flag; gates whether well-typed-but-wrong quality rules (region assignment, check density) are teachable at all under this position.

FLAG (infrastructure, not model behavior, but load-bearing and unmeasured): 'Content-addressed function-level memoized whole-program checking keeps loop latency inside an interactive teaching budget at AI-scale program sizes.' Experiment: the round-2 closed-world compile-loop pricing prototype with pre-committed reopen thresholds — this position adopts those thresholds as its own viability gate.

STANDING FLAG: all claims about how models learn a novel language from in-context spec + examples at all (including everything above) are uncarded — the corpus has zero AI-codegen evidence; per caveats.md nothing here may harden into spec without the named experiments.

result.position: Split the variable: keep the round-2 canonical regular grammar untouched (one spelling per construct, no sugar, no precedence, byte-canonical, tree-isomorphic to the checked core), but draw the LEXICON — keywords, type names, and the annotation vocabulary (fn, mut, own, i32, match, Result, Send/Sync-class capability names, wrapping/checked/saturating mode names) — from the Rust/C training distribution wherever our semantics substantially match the namesake; mint fresh names where they diverge substantially; and de-alias every borrowed name with one explicit spec line. Familiar vocabulary inside an alien-regular grammar.
result.coreArgument: 1) Alienness buys zero D2 compactness. Corrected D2's metric is rule count and uniformity, not token exoticism. Regularity lives in the GRAMMAR, and the round-2 syntax floor already maximizes it: one spelling per construct, no precedence, no elision, non-canonical input a hard error (round2-syntax 'What must be true'). Given a fixed vocabulary of N tokens, the spec defines N tokens exactly once regardless of their strings — so lexicon choice is spec-budget-neutral to first order. The maximal-regularity-even-if-alien position conflates the two variables: an alien lexicon adds not one unit of regularity to a grammar that is already fully regular by decided law. The only thing left undecided at the surface is the token strings, and there the training prior is nearly free upside whose sole cost is false-friend risk plus de-aliasing lines. 2) The lexicon is the highest-frequency surface element under the decided architecture. The owner ruling makes uniform mandatory annotation law: every signature carries ownership capability, effect row, numeric mode, ABI-visible layout (round2-synthesis TYPES AND FACTS); every allocation carries a storage contract; checks and drops are artifact-surfaced ops (round-3 envelope, Layers 0-1). Program text is therefore saturated with vocabulary tokens, and — decisively for D2 — so are the SPEC'S OWN EXAMPLES, which are written in the surface language and sit inside the in-context budget D2 protects. Familiar lexemes (fn, mut, i32, match) are single tokens in current BPE vocabularies; invented atoms fragment into multi-token sequences, inflating the taught spec+examples footprint. This is the one place lexicon choice genuinely touches the D2 budget, and it favors familiarity. (Token counts are mechanically checkable; the round2-syntax caveat about the 1.35-1.48x regex proxy stands, so this must be measured with real tokenizers.) 3) The semantics we adopted are already mostly Rust's semantics, by owner directive — so for most of the vocabulary the Rust name is the TRUE name, not a false friend. D1 names Rust-class enforcement as the reference bar; F001/F006 ownership-borrowing is the adopted model; D001-monomorphized generics are Rust's compilation model verbatim (card confirmed 2026-07-02); Result+trap=abort is Rust's error construct; exhaustive match is the dispatch floor; C004's Send/Sync-class capability predicates are literally named after Rust concepts in every round file — the design record cannot even discuss the architecture without this lexicon, and the in-context spec will be written in the same metalanguage. Refusing the namesake for a construct that IS the namesake manufactures alienness: it spends the prior and buys no spec compactness. The named-truthful-modes floor (N001/N002) is the showcase: Rust already ships an in-distribution naming scheme for exactly this vocabulary (wrapping/checked/saturating/unchecked), pre-stabilized — which matters because round2-type-inference line 30 makes annotation-vocabulary churn a systemic rewrite risk that must stabilize before mass codegen; a prior-anchored vocabulary arrives stabilized. 4) The split hedges the two opposed untested error modes round2-syntax identified. Canonical grammar eliminates precedence misbinding by construction (no precedence exists to misremember); familiar lexicon attacks the transcription-distance/recall channel. It is the only surface option that does not need to know which error rate dominates before choosing. 5) False friends are confinable, and the strict grammar is precisely the safety net that makes borrowing affordable. Where semantics diverge, the policy is threefold and each leg is one regular spec statement: (a) rename — borrow only on substantial match; (b) relocate the divergent fact into its own mandatory token — e.g., because every arithmetic node names a mode, 'i32' never implies an overflow behavior, so the most dangerous Rust habit (release-mode wrap assumption) has no silent landing site; (c) a one-line 'differs from Rust:' delta in the vocabulary table. Prior-driven misimports that emit unlicensed FORMS — '?', wildcard '_' arms, elided annotations, sugar — are grammar-rejected hard errors under the canonical-form floor. The residual silent channel is wrong-but-legal token selection, which is exactly what the false-friend experiment must measure. 6) Precedent: SPARK (F006) shipped this exact shape — a severely restricted, verification-first semantics wearing Ada's inherited familiar surface. The restriction lived in the rules, not in an alien vocabulary; round2-syntax already cites F006 as the restrict-expressiveness precedent. (Human-writer precedent; model transfer untested.) 7) Finally, the position is the only one that harvests any prior at all while respecting the floors: full familiarity (infix, precedence, sugar) is already cut and parked behind the gated codec experiment; full alienness harvests nothing. Splitting lexicon from grammar takes the entire admissible remainder of the training-distribution benefit — recorded in round2-syntax as an 'admissible, unquantified cost' of canonical form — at the price of a bounded, spec-addressable de-aliasing table.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Rust &mut exclusivity/no-alias is the adopted ownership reference bar under D1; borrowing Rust's ownership lexicon (own/mut/borrow-class names) names semantics we already imported from Rust, minimizing name-semantics mismatch by construction for the highest-frequency annotation class.

SPARK is shipped precedent for exactly this design shape: a verification-first language that restricts aliasing/ownership/side-effects at the RULE level while keeping Ada's familiar inherited surface vocabulary — restriction in rules, not alien tokens; round2-syntax already leans on F006 as the restrict-expressiveness precedent.

Confirmed 2026-07-02 verbatim: Rust generics compile by monomorphization into per-type concrete code — the decided mono-only generics floor IS Rust's model, so Rust's generics vocabulary is the true name of our construct, not a false friend.

Confirmed-with-amended-sources: Send/Sync-class capability predicates are a required kernel component, and the entire design record already names them with Rust's lexicon — evidence that this vocabulary is the natural metalanguage the in-context spec will use anyway; adopting it in-surface aligns spec, evidence base, and program text.

The named-truthful-numeric-modes floor (with N001) mandates per-operation mode names; Rust already ships an in-distribution, stable naming scheme for exactly these modes (wrapping/checked/saturating/unchecked), so the floor's vocabulary can be borrowed pre-stabilized instead of invented — and mandatory per-node mode tokens remove the silent landing site for the worst Rust habit (assumed wrap semantics).

Grounds the errors floor whose surface construct is Result<T,E> plus trap=abort (round2-synthesis line 30) — the adopted construct already carries a Rust name in the design record; renaming a construct that is semantically Rust's Result manufactures alienness for zero spec gain.

Negative scope confirmed 2026-07-02: 'Does not decide source syntax' — the evidence corpus leaves the lexicon genuinely undecided; nothing carded pushes toward alien tokens, so the choice is governed by D2 accounting and untested behavioral claims, exactly as this position frames it.

Establishes the fixed grammar half of the split (canonical form is decided law, robust to lexicon choice); records the training-distribution penalty as an 'admissible, unquantified cost'; its 'short fixed operator atoms' obligation invites familiar atoms; and its two opposed untested error modes (transcription-distance vs precedence-misbinding) are exactly what the lexicon/grammar split hedges.

Line 30: round-1 mode vocabularies must stabilize before mass code generation because annotation-vocabulary churn is systemic rewrite risk — a prior-anchored borrowed vocabulary arrives pre-stabilized with fixed meanings, while invented vocabularies churn during design.

The envelope makes annotation and check vocabulary pervasive in the artifact (checks/drops/frames surfaced as explicit ops), maximizing lexicon token frequency in both programs and the spec's in-context examples — the multiplier that makes per-token lexicon quality the dominant surface variable under D2.

concessions: De-aliasing lines are real spec mass: every borrowed name whose semantics diverge owes a 'differs from Rust:' statement, and a fully alien lexicon owes none. If the divergence census turns out large, the D2 accounting could flip against borrowing; the borrow-only-on-substantial-match policy bounds this but its per-name judgment calls are not mechanical.

The opposing position's best card is unanswered by design and only by experiment: alien tokens as 'desirable difficulty' that force spec consultation instead of prior assumption. A familiar surface may induce prior-autopilot precisely at the divergence points where the spec matters most.

Definition-by-delta ('like Rust's &mut except X') would compress the spec but makes spec correctness conditional on unverified model priors about Rust; the safe variant — fully self-contained definitions with familiar names as mnemonics only — forfeits that compression gain, so I claim spec-budget NEUTRALITY plus token-footprint savings, not spec compression, as the defensible floor.
The single-BPE-token claim is a fact about current tokenizers, not a stable guarantee across model generations; the benefit must be re-measured, not assumed permanent.
SPARK/Ada is human-writer precedent; nothing in it demonstrates that in-context model learning transfers the same way (corpus has zero AI-codegen cards; standing rule applies).

A mixed surface (familiar words, alien grammar) could in principle be uncanny-valley — activating priors the grammar then violates — and underperform BOTH pure endpoints; the position's hedging argument is structural, not measured, so the lexicon experiment must include the mixed condition, not just A/B endpoints.

Where a false friend selects a wrong-but-grammatically-legal explicit token (e.g., a mode token chosen by Rust-release habit), the canonical grammar catches nothing; this residual silent channel is conceded and is the position's principal exposure.
risks: Silent wrong-token selection at divergence points: well-typed, artifact-visible, semantically wrong choices driven by the Rust prior (mode tokens, capability names) — the one false-friend channel the strict grammar cannot convert into a hard error.

Repair-loop contamination: generated code that LOOKS like Rust invites Rust-pattern fixes during regeneration cycles, plausibly raising the correlated coherent-wrongness rate the round-2 redundancy machinery exists to catch.
Spec-reader overconfidence: a model may skim the vocabulary table because the names look known, missing exactly the de-alias deltas — the operative form of the desirable-difficulty objection.

Prior drift and version skew: the lexicon is tuned to today's models' Rust prior; future models with different priors (or a shifted Rust itself, e.g., changed aliasing model per caveats.md) inherit a vocabulary whose helpfulness was calibrated to a past distribution.
Semantic-evolution drag: if a construct's semantics later change, its familiar name becomes a standing lie and renaming is a breaking, gated vocabulary event; alien names carry no such anchor.

Design-process irregularity: per-name borrow-vs-mint decisions applied inconsistently would yield a half-familiar lexicon that is itself a mild irregularity; requires one written borrowing policy applied uniformly (borrow only on substantial semantic match, else mint), stated once.
empiricalUntested: E1 (decider) Lexicon A/B/C under identical canonical grammar and identical-length in-context spec: Rust-familiar vs alien-isomorphic vs mixed lexicon; measure first-parse success, semantic error rate, repair iterations to green, and spec-consultation behavior. Extends the round2-syntax follow-up 'measure the training-distribution penalty'; all claimed familiarity benefits are EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED until this runs.

E2 False-friend battery: tasks seeded at divergence points (no '?' operator, no '_' wildcard arms, mandatory per-node mode tokens, exhaustive match, no implicit conversions); measure unlicensed-form emission (grammar-caught) vs wrong-but-legal token selection (silent) under each lexicon. The claim that most prior misimports are grammar-caught rather than silent is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and needs a divergence census first.

E3 Tokenizer census (mechanical, run first, cheap): real-BPE token counts of spec + examples + representative programs under each lexicon across current model tokenizers; verifies the single-token claim and quantifies the D2 in-context footprint delta — replaces the flagged 1.35-1.48x regex proxy.

E4 Delta-definition vs self-contained spec definitions at matched token budget: model comprehension and downstream error rates when the spec defines borrowed constructs by delta against the Rust prior vs from scratch — decides whether the aggressive spec-compression variant is ever admissible.

E5 Prior-decay curve: how fast alien-lexicon error rates converge to familiar-lexicon rates as in-context examples accumulate (already listed as a round2-syntax follow-up); bounds the total worth of familiarity — if convergence is fast, the position's benefit is a transition subsidy, not a permanent edge.

E6 Repair-loop contamination probe: multi-turn regeneration on familiar-lexicon code with injected divergent-semantics sites; measure whether Rust-pattern fixes are imported at higher rates than under alien lexicon (tests the correlated-error risk against the round-2 flagship drift experiment's protocol).

result.position: Spec-primary: first-pass semantic correctness is the design metric. The in-context spec (compact, uniform, stated-once, with examples in the single canonical dialect) carries essentially the entire teaching burden. The interactive repair loop is retained as three things only — a safety net, a mechanical propagation executor (site-enumerated edit lists), and a fleet-scale telemetry instrument whose diagnostic-frequency ledger is read as a list of spec/language defects. Any persistent reliance on the loop to *teach* a rule is evidence of irregularity or spec incompleteness, and the fix flows to the spec or the language surface, never to more repair tooling.
result.coreArgument:
Four legs, then the toolchain obligations the topic asks for.

(1) THE SILENT-WRONG ASYMMETRY — the argument that survives even if repair loops become free. Error-driven teaching can only teach what the checker rejects, and this project's own verdicts enumerate the residual danger classes that are checker-INVISIBLE by construction: the two well-typed-but-wrong memory holes (one-big-arena pessimization, too-long-lived-region leaks — round2-synthesis line 60/74), context-dropping Result variants that type-check (round2-error-handling, AI-writer section), silent operand-order/associativity swaps that type-check and are invisible to the verifier (round2-syntax, AI-writer section), correlated-wrongness where annotation and code are both wrong and compile (round2-synthesis line 93), and performance-wrong code generally — D0's actual objective — which raises no diagnostic at all (D009 is the carded precedent that performance degradation is silent). A repair-primary pedagogy minimizes rejection rate, not wrongness rate: it systematically over-trains the model on whatever the checker catches loudest and starves exactly the classes the architecture already had to invent lint channels for — and whether writers even act on lint-class non-error feedback is itself flagged EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (round2-synthesis line 104). The curriculum for checker-invisible classes can only live in the spec and its examples, because there is no error event to hang a lesson on.

(2) THE DECIDED DIAGNOSTIC ARCHITECTURE IS REPAIR-THROUGHPUT MACHINERY, NOT A CURRICULUM. Round 2 already fixed the diagnostics design: site-enumerated mechanical edit lists (file/site/missing-arm tuples "an unattended writer can execute as a to-do list", line 69), template-level aggregation so one defect yields ONE repair site (line 66), single-hop repair naming all artifacts (line 56). These were optimized to strip deliberation out of repair. A to-do list teaches nothing generalizable — that is its virtue as a repair mechanism and its disqualification as a teacher. To make the loop pedagogical you would have to re-add explanatory content to every diagnostic, paid per-error, per-instance, per-round-trip at fleet scale — the exact opposite of D2's economics, where a rule stated once in the spec amortizes across every agent forever. Moving teaching into the loop therefore contradicts the diagnostic design the rounds already decided; spec-primary is the only position consistent with it.

(3) CORRECTED D2 FUNDS SPEC-PRIMARY DIRECTLY, AND THE LOOP BECOMES THE IRREGULARITY METER. D2's constraint is spec compactness via uniformity: few rules, no special cases, each stated once. Uniformity is simultaneously the compression device and the first-pass-correctness engine — one spelling per construct (canonical form, decided floor) means every spec example demonstrates the ONLY way to write that construct, so example transfer is maximal; zero implicit conversions, mono-only generics, no implicit dispatch each delete a whole category of "you'll learn it when the checker yells" knowledge. This yields the operational form of my assigned claim: a rule that models persistently get wrong after spec-ROI saturates is, with high probability, exactly what D2 calls irregular — context-dependent, special-cased, ambiguous. So instrument the fleet: rank diagnostic classes by fire rate; every hot diagnostic is a spec-defect or language-defect report; the remedy is a spec revision, an example, or a uniformization of the surface — never a better repair prompt. The repair loop is epistemically necessary as the *measurement instrument* for this program (conceded below), but it is the thermometer, not the medicine.

(4) FLEET ECONOMICS AND THE PROCESS-RELATIVITY OBJECTION. Repair round-trips price against channels the architecture already runs at the budget edge: the closed-world whole-program compile loop is round 2's "biggest unpriced cost" (line 98, D004 is the carded compile-time tradeoff channel), and trap=abort delivers ONE diagnostic per process death with wall-clock acceptability an explicit reopening condition (line 72; C002 the only carded post-violation observability precedent). Errors are correlated across a fleet: if N agents lack rule R in context, all N pay round-trips; one spec line amortizes to zero marginal cost. Worse, repair transcripts accumulate in the very context window that holds the spec — repair-primary teaching plausibly cannibalizes its own substrate (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, flagged). Finally, competence acquired as repair-session state is process-relative: it differs per instance, evaporates across sessions, and cannot be audited or version-pinned. The round-3 envelope's own invariant is that acceptance be artifact-checkable, not process-relative (round3-synthesis, cross-cutting invariant 4); the spec is the artifact-checkable form of writer competence, and spec-primary extends that invariant to pedagogy.

WHAT THE TOOLCHAIN MUST GUARANTEE (the topic's second half — spec-primary is not toolchain-minimal): (a) COMPLETE one-pass error reporting: all independent errors surfaced in a single check pass, deterministic local recovery, no fix-one-reveal-next serialization — so when the safety net is used it costs one round-trip, not k (extends round2-synthesis line 66). (b) RULE-CITED DIAGNOSTICS: every diagnostic carries the spec-rule ID it enforces, so the loop points back INTO the spec and reinforces it rather than substituting for it (new obligation, proposed here). (c) FLEET DIAGNOSTIC TELEMETRY as a first-class artifact: frequency-ranked diagnostic ledger feeding a pre-committed spec-revision loop, with the spec-ROI curve measured (first-pass gain per spec token added). (d) DETERMINISM: identical wrong program yields byte-identical diagnostics everywhere, so lessons are reproducible and cacheable. (e) THE ELABORATED RE-READ ARTIFACT (already law, line 67): spec examples and real code are one dialect; teaching and reading never diverge. (f) A DIFFERENTIAL-TESTING/silent-wrong harness, because the checker cannot teach what it cannot see — the curriculum for those classes must be validated by execution comparison, not rejection events. (g) PRE-COMMITTED THRESHOLDS routing persistent error mass: below-threshold first-pass rates after spec-ROI saturation trigger language redesign review (irregularity suspect) or, failing that, an honest concession of that class to the loop — bounded, named, and measured, never ambient.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Julia's carded guidance shows performance degradation from type-instability is silent — no error fires. Performance-wrong code (D0's core objective) generates no repair-loop event, so performance idioms can only be taught by the spec/examples; a repair-driven curriculum never covers them.

The carded compile-time/runtime tradeoff channel: every repair round-trip pays the closed-world whole-program check cost, and round2-synthesis line 98 names closed-world compile-loop latency as the biggest unpriced cost. Repair-primary teaching multiplies load on this exact channel.

Monomorphization's compile-time cost made iteration-loop latency a carded, first-class AI cost channel (cited as such by round2-syntax 'What must be true'); content-addressed caching was justified to MINIMIZE loop cost — evidence the architecture treats round-trips as a cost to shrink, not a channel to load teaching onto.

The only carded post-violation observability precedent (halt-on-detection), underwriting trap=abort: one diagnostic per process death (round2-synthesis line 72). Runtime-error-driven teaching is maximally expensive under the decided failure semantics; lessons must land at or before check time — i.e., in the spec.

LLVM no-alias facts are sound only if frontend obligations are true — the carded proof that wrongness invisible to downstream checking exists by construction at the fact-emission boundary. A checker-driven curriculum structurally cannot teach the classes the checker cannot see.

SPARK is carded precedent that verification-first languages restrict surface expressiveness to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — the same restriction move that makes a spec compact, uniform, and completely teachable up front, supporting spec-primary's feasibility.

InstCombine's discipline prices every rewrite rule at proof-grade validation cost — the verification analog of D2's rule: each surface irregularity costs both spec budget and verification budget. Uniformity pays twice, and uniformity is the first-pass-correctness engine spec-primary relies on.

The decided diagnostics design — site-enumerated edit lists as executable to-do lists, template-level aggregation to one repair site, single-hop repair — is repair-throughput machinery deliberately stripped of explanatory content; it cannot double as a curriculum, so teaching must live in the spec channel.

The mandatory elaborated re-read artifact makes spec examples and real code one canonical dialect — the toolchain guarantee that maximizes in-context example transfer, which is the load-bearing mechanism of spec-primary teaching.

Documents the silent, type-checking operand-order/associativity error class invisible to the verifier, and mandates canonical single-spelling form plus content-addressed caching — both the silent-wrong asymmetry and the one-dialect teaching substrate come from this ruling.

Names context-dropping Result variants as 'a canonical silent-drift class because context-dropping variants type-check' — a decided-language example of wrongness that never fires a diagnostic, hence never triggers a repair-loop lesson; the remedy chosen (auto-derived context) was by-construction language design, not repair tooling — the spec-primary pattern applied.

'Acceptance is artifact-checkable, not process-relative' is decided architecture; spec-primary extends the same invariant to pedagogy — competence encoded in a version-pinned spec is artifact-checkable, competence accumulated as per-instance repair-session state is process-relative and unauditable.

The owner ruling that spec compactness via uniformity is the constraint and uniform verbose-everywhere rules are spec-cheap directly funds spec-primary: uniformity is simultaneously the compression device and the first-pass-correctness mechanism, and persistent repair-loop hot spots are irregularity detectors under D2's own definition.

concessions: The repair loop is irreducible as a MEASUREMENT instrument: first-pass correctness cannot be known, and the spec-ROI/telemetry program I propose cannot run, without executing and instrumenting the loop. Spec-primary needs the loop as its QA channel; my claim is it must never be the curriculum, not that it must not exist.

Mechanical propagation via site-enumerated edit lists (variant added, contract changed) is decided, correct, and cheap; I do not oppose repair as an EXECUTOR of closed-set changes. My position targets teaching-by-error, not O(K) mechanical propagation.

Ownership/borrowing errors are genuinely nonlocal, and no known in-context teaching guarantees high first-pass rates on them for a novel language — models fight borrow checkers even in Rust, which saturates their training data. Some bounded, named repair-loop reliance for this class is plausibly irreducible; spec-primary must concede it explicitly rather than demand infinite redesign.

The economic leg is unpriced in both directions: if the closed-world memoized compile loop (round2-synthesis line 98 experiment, not yet run) proves round-trips near-free at fleet scale, the wall-clock argument weakens substantially. The silent-wrong asymmetry leg is independent of loop cost and stands regardless.

D2's fixed spec budget bounds my own program: spec-primary cannot chase first-pass correctness by adding rules and examples indefinitely. When the spec-ROI curve saturates, the residual error mass belongs either to language redesign or — honestly — to the loop.

If the head-to-head pedagogy experiment shows models learn a novel language better per token from interactive error feedback than from spec text, my core premise fails and burden allocation must be renegotiated; I pre-commit to that experiment as the decider rather than claiming its outcome.
risks: Goodhart on my own metric: 'first-pass correctness' measured as checker acceptance creates pressure to weaken the checker or over-annotate until things pass. The metric must be defined as first-pass SEMANTIC correctness via differential testing, or spec-primary optimizes rejection-avoidance — the same failure mode I attribute to repair-primary.

Spec bloat self-defeat: treating every fleet hot-spot as a spec gap invites adding clarifications, examples, and edge-case notes until the spec violates D2. The discipline must be uniformization (fewer rules) over elaboration (more text), and that discipline is not self-enforcing.

Shipping paralysis: 'any repair reliance is a language-design defect' taken literally blocks release forever, because some error classes will never reach threshold. Without pre-committed thresholds and an explicit concession path, spec-primary becomes an unfalsifiable demand for endless redesign.

Spec version churn: telemetry-driven spec revision means the teaching artifact keeps changing; every revision re-teaches the fleet, invalidates cached prefixes, and makes cross-version behavior comparisons murky. Spec stability and spec improvement are in direct tension and need an explicit cadence policy.

Unauthorized toolchain surface: rule-cited diagnostics, the fleet diagnostic ledger, and the spec-ROI harness are new obligations no round has authorized; they add toolchain complexity and their own drift/maintenance burden — the near-duplicate-machinery risk the charter polices.

Bias transfer rather than bias removal: spec-primary concentrates teaching power in whoever writes the spec examples; a skewed example set biases the whole fleet at once, with no per-instance error signal to correct it — a single point of pedagogical failure the distributed repair loop does not have.
empiricalUntested: That a compact spec + canonical examples in context achieves acceptable first-pass semantic correctness on a genuinely novel language at all (corpus has ZERO AI-codegen evidence; standing round-2 flag). Experiment E1 — pedagogy A/B: identical kernel tasks under (a) full compressed spec, no repair; (b) minimal spec + k repair rounds; (c) full spec + repair; measure first-pass semantic correctness by differential testing, iterations-to-green, tokens, wall-clock.

That error-driven teaching biases writers toward checker-visible error classes and Goodharts annotations into checker-satisfying but semantically empty statements (my central asymmetry claim, stated as directional structure, not measured). Experiment E2 — silent-wrong battery: seed tasks whose natural failure is well-typed-wrong (region pessimization, context-dropping chains, operand-order swaps); compare silent-wrong rates of spec-taught vs repair-taught writers; also test whether repair-taught writers' redundancy annotations retain independence (checksum value) or collapse toward checker-satisfaction.

That accumulated repair transcripts degrade effective in-context spec retention (context-churn cannibalization). Experiment E5 — measure first-pass correctness on task t+1 as a function of accumulated repair-transcript length from tasks 1..t, spec position held constant.

That uniform, no-special-case rules are learned better in-context than irregular ones — D2's own load-bearing assumption, which my position inherits. Extend the round2-syntax A/B's training-distribution-penalty measurement: matched rule sets differing only in regularity, measure first-parse and first-pass semantic error rates and their decay with in-context examples.

That AI writers act effectively on lint-class (non-error) feedback in-loop — already flagged at round2-synthesis line 104; decisive for whether the loop can cover even the lint-visible fraction of silent-wrong classes.

That rule-cited diagnostics improve repair generalization (fewer repeat offenses per rule) versus bare edit lists. Experiment E4b — same defect distribution, diagnostics with vs without spec-rule citations; measure repeat-offense rate per rule class across subsequent tasks.

That the repair loop's fleet wall-clock is acceptable under closed-world compilation and the multi-process trap=abort harness (round2-synthesis lines 72 and 98 experiments, both pending); prices the economic leg of my argument in either direction.

That the spec-ROI curve saturates — that first-pass gain per added spec token declines to a measurable knee, which my threshold-and-concede policy presupposes. Experiment E3 — iterative telemetry-driven spec revision targeting top-k hot diagnostics; measure first-pass improvement per spec token added until the knee appears (or fails to).

result.position: Regularity-first: the canonical surface maximizes uniformity — one construct shape, one spelling per semantic node, one byte-level formatting, grammar isomorphic to the checked tree — even where that form is alien to training distributions. Training-familiar conventions are admissible only as isolated lexemes carrying zero rule structure (keywords, delimiters); familiar GRAMMAR (precedence, statement/expression split, sugar, elision) is excluded from core. Any familiar rendering enters later, if ever, as the already-parked gated bijective write-codec over the fixed canonical core — tooling, not language.
result.coreArgument: (1) Corrected D2's metric is spec description length, and on that metric regularity-first is not one contender among two — it is the objective's argmin. The true description of a surface is its grammar productions plus precedence/associativity tables plus sugar expansions plus elision/inference rules plus every special case, each stated once, plus the examples needed to teach it in context. A perfectly uniform form has the smallest true description because it has one construct shape and no exception clauses. A Rust/C-adjacent surface cannot deliver familiarity without importing the rule mass that makes text read as Rust/C: a 13+-level precedence table, statement/expression duality, method-call vs free-call duality, pattern and control-flow sugar, elision rules. Every one of those is on D2's named spend list verbatim ('sugar, alternate spellings, inference rules, context-dependent elision, special cases'). Note also what corrected D2 removed: the round-4 v1 framing (program-token budget as first-class) is void (user-directives.md line 20; workflow-failures.jsonl 2026-07-02 'superseded'), so familiarity's token-compactness argument is owner-dead; its ONLY surviving argument is behavioral prior-leverage, which sits entirely in the EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED bucket (zero AI-codegen cards — round-2 standing flag, caveats.md standing rule). Regularity's spec-mass advantage, by contrast, is computable today without a model in the loop: write both candidate grammar specs and count productions and exception clauses. Half this debate is decidable by arithmetic. (2) Importing familiar grammar re-imports frontend soundness surface, not just spec mass. Every desugaring and precedence-resolution rule is a frontend transformation whose semantics-preservation must be proven and maintained at the pipeline's flagged soundness chokepoint (F003 negative scope; misleading-comparisons #5: IR facts are sound only if frontend semantics are sound; N006 prices each rewrite at Alive2 proof grade). A zero-sugar core incurs zero such obligations; F006/SPARK is carded precedent that verification-first languages restrict surface expressiveness and win by it. (3) The failure-mode asymmetry is decidable structurally even before experiments. The whole D0/D1 architecture converts writer errors into loud machine-repairable diagnostics; its one acknowledged blind spot is coherent wrongness that typechecks (round2-type-inference correlated-wrongness; round2-syntax silent operand-order/precedence-misbinding classes; D009 as the canonical silent-failure exhibit the project already weights as disqualifying). A regular-alien surface fails LOUD: unfamiliarity surfaces as parse/check errors routed into the repair loop the toolchain is built around. A familiar-divergent surface fails SILENT: the model's prior fills unstated gaps with Rust/C semantics precisely where this language deliberately diverges — overflow is wrap-legal policy here (N002; round-3 Layer 1) vs Rust's debug-panic/release-wrap; zero implicit conversions vs Rust's deref/unsizing coercions; explicit instantiation vs inferred generics (D001, CONFIRMED 2026-07-02); no closures, no method dispatch, Result+trap=abort. A '+' that reads as Rust's '+' but lowers to a different per-operation contract produces a program that is well-formed, self-consistent, and wrong only relative to intent — invisible to every checksum channel (redundancy, elaboration diffing) because those catch inconsistency, not coherent wrongness. Alien, mode-carrying spellings put the divergent fact in the token instead of in the prior — the same 'make performance-critical semantics visible to tools' lesson F009 cards for SIL, and consistent with C004's amended scope showing even Rust-trait-shaped intuitions mislead about what is and is not guaranteed. The MAGNITUDE of both effects is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the DETECTABILITY asymmetry is architectural: one failure class lands in the diagnostics channel, the other in the void, and a project whose decisive axis is verification (round2-syntax verdict) must prefer the failure mode its machinery can catch. (4) Regularity is the substrate the decided architecture already load-bears on: the unified consistency layer (content-addressed subtree hashing, elaboration diffing, one-definition rules, provenance diffs), node-path diagnostics, deterministic O(n) parsing with local recovery, and content-addressed caching (the carded compile-time channel, D001/D004) all require byte-canonical single-spelling form; any familiar surface that lets one semantic tree own several legal byte forms degrades byte-identity to normalized-tree-identity and grows the toolchain and the spec together. (5) Steelman refinement that removes the strawman: this position bans familiar rule STRUCTURE, not familiar LEXEMES. Where a familiar token is exactly one rule with no exceptions (braces, 'fn', 'let', '->', comma lists), adopting it costs zero spec and is weakly positive; alienness is never a goal, only uniformity is. (6) Regularity-first is the no-regret ordering. The round-2 syntax verdict already parks the escape valve: a bijective, version-pinned, parse-print-parse-fixpoint write-codec admissible behind the gated A/B experiment — familiarity purchasable later as a renderer at zero spec cost if prior-leverage ever cards out. The reverse ordering does not exist: bake C precedence and Rust-shaped sugar into the canonical form now, and adverse silent-error data later forces a language break that invalidates every stored artifact and every in-context teaching example. Under symmetric ignorance about model behavior, take the branch that keeps the option and whose worst case is loud.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-grade promises sound only if frontend obligations hold; every precedence/sugar/elision rule a familiar surface imports is additional frontend transformation surface at exactly this chokepoint. Fewer grammar rules = smaller trust path.

SPARK is carded precedent that a verification-first language deliberately restricts surface expressiveness (anti-aliasing, side-effect-free expressions) to keep the source-to-logic mapping simple — the regularity-first move, already validated in deployment.

Swift SIL's lesson: performance-critical semantics should be visible to tools, not buried in implicit forms. Alien mode-carrying spellings put per-node contracts in the token; familiar surfaces hide them behind conventions the prior 'already knows' — wrongly, where we diverge.

The undefinedness ladder (UB/poison/undef/freeze) is the real semantics of record and matches no C/Rust surface intuition; a familiar surface advertises contracts models think they know, misrepresenting the ones that actually govern.

Unflagged add wraps; nsw/nuw are separate optimizer promises. This is the concrete silent-prior exhibit: a Rust-looking '+' under our wrap-legal policy (round-3 Layer 1) invites models to import Rust's panic/wrap split. One truthful mode name per node, in an unfamiliar spelling, is the defense.

InstCombine's Alive2 discipline prices every rewrite rule at proof grade; each piece of familiar sugar is a rewrite the toolchain must prove and maintain forever. Zero-sugar surface = zero such obligations.

CONFIRMED 2026-07-02. Mono-only generics with explicit instantiation arguments is decided floor; Rust-familiar syntax would invite the inference-shaped generic expectations (turbofish-optional, bound solving) this language explicitly rejects — a semantics/surface mismatch by construction.

The corpus's canonical silent-degradation exhibit, already used across round 2 to establish that silent failure modes are the disqualifying class. The silent-prior-error hazard is that same class arising at the surface layer.

CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02, scope amended: data races prevented, general race conditions not. Demonstrates that even for Rust itself, trait-shaped familiarity misleads about guarantee scope — importing Rust's spelling imports its overclaims.

Decided verdict: exactly one canonical form, sugar/infix/precedence cut, non-canonical input a hard error; the infix codec parked behind a gated A/B experiment. Regularity-first is the direct continuation of decided law; familiarity-first would reopen a closed verdict against its own gating condition.

The unified consistency layer (subtree hashing, elaboration diffing, one-definition rules, provenance diffs) is specified over the byte-canonical single-spelling artifact; six verdicts depend on it. Familiar surface multiplicity (several byte forms per tree) degrades its substrate.

D2 names the spend list — sugar, alternate spellings, inference, context-dependent elision, special cases — which is precisely the inventory of what makes Rust/C surfaces feel familiar; and it voids the program-token argument that was familiarity's strongest remaining leg (round-4 v1 superseded, workflow-failures.jsonl).

concessions: The one measured cost points against me directionally: the round-2 token proxy showed S-expression-style canonical form costs 1.35-1.48x proxy tokens and 1.93x characters vs infix on expression-dense fragments. Corrected D2 prices this as acceptable by owner ruling, but context-window pressure and generation latency are real channels, and whole-program real-tokenizer numbers do not exist.

A truly alien form plausibly has a higher first-exposure defect rate before in-context examples close the gap. If the familiarity penalty decays slowly or never, regularity-first pays a persistent generation-error tax. I cannot rule this out; only the named decay experiment can.

An alien surface reduces but does not eliminate prior leakage: priors operate at the semantic level, not just the notational level. The primary defense against prior-import is the already-decided mandatory-explicit-facts floor; surface alienness is a second-order reinforcement, not the load-bearing wall.

Familiar lexemes with no rule structure (braces, 'fn', 'let', '->') are spec-neutral and should be adopted where exactly as regular; a maximally-alien-vocabulary reading of my position would overreach the D2 metric and I disclaim it.

D2's effective in-context budget includes teaching examples. If the alien form requires materially more examples to reach the same fluency, its spec+examples footprint could exceed a familiar surface's smaller-example footprint — a genuine D2-internal counterargument to my position, currently untested.

Alien-uniform designs historically accrete their own irregularities (Lisp reader macros, quote sugar are the cautionary precedent). Uniformity is a property that must be policed by the decided toolchain-boundary rules; alienness per se guarantees nothing.

My headline hazard (silent prior-import under familiar-divergent surfaces) is itself unmeasured in magnitude — the asymmetry argument I rest on is about detectability of the failure classes, not about their measured rates.
risks: Persistent-tax risk: if the familiarity penalty does not decay with in-context examples across model generations, every program forever costs more repair iterations than a Rust-adjacent surface would have.
Example-budget blowup: teaching the alien form may consume more of the in-context window than the grammar rules it saved, inverting the D2 arithmetic in practice.

Ecosystem isolation: no off-the-shelf tooling (parsers, highlighters, diff/merge tools, LSPs) understands the alien form; all tooling is bespoke and lands on the project's critical path — a schedule risk, not a soundness risk, but real under D0's compile-loop cost channel.

Codec-tension risk: if the gated infix write-codec is later admitted, the elaborator-as-codec situation round 2 flagged (second-spelling hazard, bijectivity/fixpoint obligations, source-of-record ambiguity during repair loops) must be engineered correctly or the single-spelling guarantee silently un-cuts itself.

Moving-target risk: prior-leverage and penalty-decay are properties of specific model generations; any experimental verdict has a shelf life and the surface decision is the hardest one to reverse after artifacts accumulate.

Metric-overfit risk: minimizing grammar rule count can push complexity into a large uniform node vocabulary (hundreds of opcodes/modes); shape uniformity does not cap vocabulary size, and vocabulary also spends spec and teaching budget. The D2 accounting must count both.
empiricalUntested: Prior-leverage claim (opponent's core): models produce fewer defects writing training-familiar surfaces than alien-uniform ones. UNTESTED — zero AI-codegen cards. Experiment: the round-2 syntax A/B (identical kernel specs rendered to canonical vs Rust-adjacent form, real BPE tokenizers, whole programs; first-parse success, silent semantic-error rate via differential testing, repair iterations to green).

Silent prior-import claim (my core hazard): familiar syntax with divergent semantics induces models to import Rust/C semantics (e.g., assuming panic-on-overflow where wrap is law, assuming deref coercion, assuming generic inference). UNTESTED. Experiment: extend the syntax A/B with semantic-divergence traps — surfaces isomorphic to Rust but governed by our floors; differential-test the rate of imported-semantics errors under familiar vs alien rendering, classed by whether any checker catches them.

Penalty-decay claim: the alien form's initial defect rate converges to the familiar form's within a small number of in-context examples. UNTESTED. Experiment: teachability-decay curves — defect rate vs example count per surface (already a round-2 syntax follow-up), run per model generation.

Uniformity-generalization claim (my supporting mechanism): one construct shape makes each in-context example generalize further, so a uniform grammar needs fewer examples per construct. UNTESTED. Experiment: ablate example count and construct coverage inside the decay-curve battery.

Spec-consultation mechanism: alien notation forces the model to consult the in-context spec rather than fill from prior. UNTESTED mechanism hypothesis; operationalize indirectly as the error-category shift (prior-shaped errors vs spec-lookup errors) between surfaces in the divergence-trap experiment.

Explicitly NOT model-behavioral and computable now: the D2 spec-mass comparison itself — draft both grammar specs (uniform-alien vs Rust-adjacent) and count productions, precedence levels, sugar expansions, and exception clauses. Run this first; it can settle the spec-budget half of the debate before any model experiment.

result.position: ADOPT the hard budget as a formal decision gate: the complete writable-subset spec (grammar + typing/ownership rules + annotation vocabulary + canonical examples) must fit a fixed, CI-enforced token budget of order 10-20k tokens (Lua/Scheme-class anchor, provisionally set, calibrated by a pre-committed floor-pricing audit before enforcement hardens). Spec-token delta and rule-count delta become gate criteria in implementation/decision-gates.md alongside optimizer/verification necessity. Any construct whose whole-spec differential cost breaks the budget is cut or restructured as toolchain behavior with a thin writer-visible surface — with the single carve-out that the gate ranks constructs and mechanism choices, never D1 invariants, round-2 floors, or the round-3 envelope, which are priced first as a kernel reserve.
result.coreArgument:
1) D2 WITHOUT A NUMBER IS UNENFORCEABLE, AND THIS PROJECT'S OWN METHOD SAYS SO.
