The corrected D2 ruling already names 'spec description-length/regularity' the FIRST-CLASS METRIC (notes/user-directives.md; decision-gates.md D2 section). A first-class metric that gates nothing is decoration: every future debate will argue its construct is 'uniform enough,' and spec mass will accrete the way the round-2 synthesis documents it already accreting — 'the periphery is accreting into a second, evidence-free language' and 'six verdicts each mandated their own drift mechanism' (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md, Open tensions). The corpus method for preventing exactly this failure is the hard gate: caveats.md's standing rule (cite exact cards or it does not ship) and the decision-gates table ('a feature cannot become a language default merely because it is optimizer-attractive'). The budget is the same move applied to D2: a construct cannot enter the writable subset merely because it is optimizer- or verification-ATTRACTIVE; it must also pay its description-length cost inside a fixed envelope. Round 2 ran on a hard null hypothesis (CUT unless necessary); D2 currently has no equivalent hard operator. This position supplies it.

2) THE GATE RETRODICTS ROUND 2, WHICH IS EVIDENCE IT TRACKS THE RIGHT QUALITY. Score every round-2 verdict by spec cost and the budget gate reproduces the verdict lattice almost exactly: everything cut is spec-expensive by D2's own definition of what eats budget (macros need hygiene/staging/second-evaluation-semantics rules; exceptions need unwinding semantics plus cleanup invariants; sugar needs alternate spellings plus a precedence table; inference needs inference rules plus context-dependent elision; implicit conversions need a coercion lattice), and everything kept or mandated as replacement is a few uniform rules at the writer surface with the mass relocated to uncounted toolchain machinery (external generators + provenance diffing instead of macros, round2-metaprogramming.md; auto-derived Result context instead of unwinding; compiler-derived constant facts instead of writer-asserted ones; canonical form instead of grammar variants, round2-syntax.md). Round 2 reached these results by expensive adversarial deliberation; the budget compresses that deliberation into a cheap, continuous, reusable criterion available at every future design decision — including the open tensions where the interfaces verdict says the alternatives are 'empirically unarbitrable today.' There, spec-cost is the only measurable tie-breaker that exists NOW: contract groups vs bounded quantification, the closures escape valve, the sharing axis can each be priced in rules-and-tokens this week, versus an experiment program that is expensive, pending, and partly blocked on missing cards.

3) THE BUDGET IS DUAL-SUPPORTED: EVEN IF EVERY MODEL-LEARNING CLAIM FAILS, IT STILL BUYS THE CORPUS'S DECISIVE AXIS. Description length is not only a bet about in-context learning; it is a proxy for trusted-surface size, which is the axis the corpus actually decided round 2 on. N006 prices every rule and flag-sensitive fold at Alive2 proof-grade validation cost — rule count is literally a multiplier on the verification program. F003's negative scope makes every frontend rule soundness-critical (facts are sound only if frontend obligations hold and survive lowering); the syntax verdict already ruled on exactly this basis. F006 (SPARK) is the corpus's confirmed precedent that RESTRICTING semantics is the route to true-by-construction facts. And F001 shows what optimizers actually consume: a small uniform vocabulary of semantic facts (exclusivity, non-interference), not a wide construct surface — so cutting spec mass forfeits no carded optimization enabler (the syntax verdict: 'no card attributes any performance benefit' to surface richness). A hard budget therefore fails safe: its untested benefit is model accuracy; its carded benefit is a smaller frontend soundness chokepoint and fewer proof-grade obligations.

4) THE DEPLOYMENT MODE MAKES THE BOUND REAL WHETHER WE ADOPT IT OR NOT. D2 fixes the teaching channel: in-context spec + examples. That context is shared with everything round 2 added as mandatory context consumers — the fully-elaborated re-read artifact, site-enumerated edit lists, machine-readable diagnostics, provenance records, interface views (round2-synthesis 'Additions required because the writer is an AI'). The spec's context share is therefore bounded in practice; the only choice is whether the bound is designed (a budget with cut authority) or discovered (a spec that silently no longer fits alongside the program being repaired). Choosing beats discovering. The learnability curve against spec size is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and flagged below with named experiments — but note the asymmetry: the anti-budget position needs the curve to be FLAT (spec size never matters), which is a stronger untested claim than 'a bound exists.'

5) WHAT THE BUDGET FORCES ARCHITECTURALLY — and each consequence is independently aligned with decided law: (a) A TWO-LEDGER ARCHITECTURE: the counted writable-subset spec vs the uncounted toolchain/derived ledger; every construct proposal must declare which ledger each obligation lands on. This generalizes the pattern round 2 already used three times (metaprogramming→external generators, modules→generated interface views, error-handling→auto-derived context) from an ad-hoc move into the default design operator: spec-expensive machinery is restructured as toolchain behavior with a thin writer-visible surface (A006 is the carded precedent — Halide relocates optimization power into declarative structure plus external search, no staged surface). (b) KERNEL RESERVE PRICED FIRST: tokenize the round-2 floors and round-3 envelope before anything competes for the remainder; C004 is direct evidence D1 fits a small budget — Rust's data-race guarantee is delivered by a tiny type-level rule set (Send/Sync + &mut exclusivity), and the round-3 envelope's 'no writer-emittable third state' is one rule. If the floors alone bust 20k, the position fails falsifiably and the number is re-derived in public — a feature, not a bug: it makes D2 testable. (c) ONE ANNOTATION GRAMMAR: ownership, modes, effects, contracts, and storage annotations share a single uniform vocabulary; per-feature bespoke syntax is budget-punished, forcing the orthogonality D2 demands (D009 supports pricing context-dependence out: hidden, context-dependent behavior is the carded silent-degradation channel). (d) WHOLE-SPEC DIFFERENTIAL ACCOUNTING: the gate operates on delta(total spec), not per-construct cost, so a construct that PAYS for itself by deleting N ad-hoc rules elsewhere (e.g., contract groups replacing per-feature conformance prose) is correctly kept — this answers the naive objection that budgets always favor cutting. (e) PERIPHERY PRICING: the FFI wall's writer-visible surface counts against the budget, converting the synthesis's named accretion risk into a visible, gated cost. (f) SPEC-AS-CI-ARTIFACT from day one: token/rule-count deltas on every design change, a budget column in decision-gates.md, and a no-shadow-spec rule (no normative content outside the counted artifact) that mirrors the syntax verdict's no-second-spelling policing. The spec artifact this forces into existence is also the missing prerequisite for the experiments round 2 already ordered — the syntax A/B, the drift experiment, and every in-context trial need a bounded teachable spec to run at all.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: SPARK is the corpus's confirmed precedent that verification-first languages RESTRICT semantics to a small checkable fragment to get true-by-construction facts; the budget is a quantified restriction-forcing gate in exactly that tradition.

InstCombine's discipline prices flag-sensitive folds at Alive2 proof-grade validation; rule count is a direct cost multiplier on the verification program, so description length proxies verifier obligations — the budget's carded (non-model-dependent) payoff.

noalias/alias.scope facts are UB-grade promises sound only if frontend obligations hold; every writable-surface rule is frontend soundness-chokepoint mass, so a token/rule budget bounds the trusted transformation surface.
What optimizers consume is a small uniform vocabulary of semantic facts (exclusivity, non-interference), not surface richness — so budget-driven surface cuts forfeit no carded optimization enabler.
Rust's data-race guarantee rides a tiny type-level rule set (Send/Sync auto-traits + &mut exclusivity) — direct evidence the D1 kernel reserve plausibly fits inside a Lua/Scheme-class budget.

Monomorphization-only generics with explicit instantiation is decided law and is the spec-cheap uniform mechanism (one expansion rule, no bound-solving/inference rules); the budget gate retrodicts this floor.

Halide's schedule layer shows optimization power relocated into declarative in-language structure plus external search — the carded precedent for the budget's 'move to toolchain' arm without spec-expensive staging semantics.

Julia's Function-typed fields and abstract types show hidden, context-dependent behavior is the silent-degradation channel; supports the budget's pricing rule that context-dependence and elision are the expensive spec items.

Canonical-form-only ruling plus its toolchain-boundary policing ('no second stored spelling') is the template for the no-shadow-spec rule; it also concedes the training-distribution penalty as admissible-unquantified — an honest cost the budget position inherits.

Replace-with-alternative via deterministic external generators + provenance diffing is the existing spec-to-toolchain relocation the budget generalizes: writer-visible rules stay few, machinery lives outside the counted spec.

Its Open tensions (periphery accreting into 'a second, evidence-free language'; six uncoordinated drift mechanisms) document what an uncounted ledger produces — the failure mode the budget makes visible and gateable.

The gate-table method ('a feature cannot become a default merely because it is optimizer-attractive') plus the D2-corrected ruling naming description-length/regularity the first-class metric — the budget operationalizes that metric with cut authority.

D2 corrected: spec compactness is the constraint and IRREGULARITY is what eats budget — the budget's accounting rules (rules/special-cases counted, uniform verbosity cheap) are read directly off the directive.

The standing rule (exact-card citation or it does not ship) establishes that this project governs itself by hard, checkable gates; the budget extends the same governance to D2 instead of leaving it as advocacy.

concessions: The 10-20k number is an un-carded analogy: no one has tokenized Lua 5.4 or R7RS-small with a real BPE tokenizer, and full reference manuals for both would likely exceed 20k tokens — the anchor is defensible only for a writable-subset spec (grammar + rules + annotation vocabulary + canonical examples), and the number must be calibrated by pricing the round-2/3 floors before the gate acquires cut authority. Until then it is hard-in-procedure (every change carries a counted delta), not hard-in-threshold.

The assigned phrase 'regardless of round-1/2 merit' must be read down: the gate ranks constructs and mechanism choices, never invariants. D1, the round-2 floors, and the round-3 envelope are fixed law and are priced first as kernel reserve; the budget chooses among D1-satisfying designs and cuts merely-beneficial constructs. A budget that could cut safety rules would violate the debate's own fixed constraints.

Token count is gameable: prose compression, terser wording, and normative content smuggled into 'toolchain documentation' the model still needs all reduce the counted number without reducing complexity. Rule/production count must be a co-equal metric and a no-shadow-spec rule (no normative content outside the counted artifact) is a required companion, or Goodhart wins.

Spec-cheap is not proven model-friendly: a uniform but training-distribution-alien surface may be compact and still error-prone — round2-syntax.md explicitly admits the training-distribution penalty as an admissible, unquantified cost. The budget optimizes description length, which D2 asserts (but no card proves) is the binding variable.

A hard gate applied today could pre-empt the two needs_evidence verdicts (interior-annotation regime, interfaces/bounded-quantification): for those topics the budget must enter as a pre-registered input to their experiments' decision criteria, not as an override that decides them before the evidence arrives.

Description length under-measures rule INTERACTION complexity: k orthogonal rules can be spec-cheap yet generate combinatorially many semantic corner cases. The budget needs a companion special-case/interaction audit; it is a necessary metric, not a sufficient one.
risks: Wrong-number risk: if the priced kernel reserve (round-2 floors + round-3 envelope + FFI declaration frames) alone approaches or busts the ceiling, either necessary machinery gets blocked or the number moves — and a moved number permanently weakens the gate's 'hard' credibility. Mitigation is sequencing: floor-pricing audit BEFORE the gate acquires cut authority; but the residual risk of a recalibration spiral is real.

Complexity displacement: because the toolchain ledger is uncounted, the gate systematically pressures designs toward toolchain relocation, and the synthesis already warns the toolchain is reproducing near-duplicate drift machinery (six uncoordinated mechanisms) and the periphery is accreting into a second language. Without a policing rule for the uncounted ledger, the budget could relocate complexity to exactly where adversarial review is weakest.

Tie-breaking authority may outrun evidence: using spec-cost to break the generics/interfaces and closures/dyn tensions is decisive but could lock in the wrong mechanism if the pending experiments would have gone the other way; every budget-broken tie needs a recorded reopening condition tied to the experiment it pre-empted.

Retrieval substitution: if tooling lets the writer consult the spec on demand (RAG-style) rather than holding it fully in context, the in-context sizing rationale weakens to the verification-surface rationale alone, and the specific 10-20k number loses its justification and must be re-derived from verifier-cost grounds.

Late-arriving surface demands: concurrency surface (C004 kernel undesigned), FFI attenuation (research_needed, zero primary-source cards), and region/arena contracts are unpriced; if they arrive after the budget is allocated, the gate forces either painful re-cuts of already-adopted constructs or another credibility-damaging ceiling move.

Example starvation: examples count against the budget, and if the examples-vs-rules experiment shows examples do most of the teaching, a budget tuned to minimize rules could produce a spec that is compact but pedagogically starved — optimizing the wrong term of the in-context payload.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: models write a novel language more accurately when its full spec + examples fit in context, with degradation as spec size grows. Experiment: SPEC-SIZE ABLATION — teach the same kernel via spec variants at ~5k/10k/20k/40k tokens with fixed example budget; measure first-parse success, floor-violation rate (ownership/mode/annotation errors), and repair iterations to green.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: irregularity (special cases, alternate spellings, context-dependent rules) drives model error more than raw spec token count — the causal core of corrected D2. Experiment: REGULARITY-VS-SIZE ABLATION — same language specified twice at matched token count, one uniform, one with N special cases; compare error taxonomies.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: marginal spec tokens displace useful context (program text, elaborated views, edit lists, diagnostics) with measurable accuracy cost in realistic repair loops. Experiment: CONTEXT-COMPETITION — fixed window, vary spec share against repair-task payload at AI-scale program sizes; measure repair success per 1k spec tokens.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: the optimal rules-vs-examples split of a fixed in-context budget. Experiment: EXAMPLES-RATIO ABLATION — same total budget allocated 80/20, 50/50, 20/80 between normative rules and worked canonical examples; measure writing accuracy per token. Outcome directly parameterizes whether canonical examples should be counted inside or reserved beside the budget.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: on-demand spec retrieval substitutes for in-context presence at equal accuracy (would narrow the budget rationale to verification-surface benefits only). Experiment: RETRIEVAL-SUBSTITUTION — spec-in-context vs spec-via-lookup-tool on identical tasks; measure accuracy and loop latency.

MEASUREMENT MISSING (not model behavior, but currently un-carded and load-bearing for the anchor): real-BPE token counts of Lua 5.4 reference manual, R7RS-small, and a drafted round-2/3 floors spec — the CALIBRATION AUDIT that must run before the 10-20k threshold acquires cut authority. No spec-size research lane currently exists in notes/missing-research-backlog.jsonl; one must be opened with new card IDs, per the caveats.md standing rule that every behavioral claim here is otherwise uncardable.

result.position: Familiarity-first surface WITHIN the fixed regularity floors: wherever the decided core's semantics genuinely match the Rust/C-family prior (checked ownership/borrowing, Result+match exhaustiveness, mono-only generics with explicit instantiation, zero implicit conversions), the single canonical spelling that the round-2 syntax floor mandates anyway should be the prior's spelling — Rust-adjacent keywords (fn/let/struct/enum/match), familiar operator atoms with mandatory mode qualifiers, brace/arrow structure, and rustfmt-like canonical bytes. Every semantic DELTA from the prior (per-node numeric modes, effect rows, storage/lifetime contracts, env-structs, foreign-shared fields, capability predicates) gets a visibly novel spelling. Governing rule, stated once (D2-cheap): the PRIOR-ALIGNMENT INVARIANT — a familiar spelling may be used only where checked semantics match the prior's semantics; no familiar token ever carries unfamiliar meaning. This is not a bid to reopen sugar, precedence, elision, or alternate spellings: those cuts are law (round2-syntax, round2-synthesis SURFACE line) and familiarity-first operates strictly inside them.
result.coreArgument: The debate's framing is a false dichotomy. Regularity is a property of the RULE SET; familiarity is a property of the LEXEME AND NODE-SHAPE INVENTORY. The round-2 floors already fix the rule set at maximum regularity: exactly one spelling per construct, one legal byte formatting, grammar isomorphic to the checked tree, no sugar, no precedence, no elision, non-canonical input a hard error. The only decision left is WHICH single spelling each construct gets — and per construct, the spec states that spelling exactly once regardless of which token is chosen. Choosing the prior-matching token is therefore approximately spec-mass-neutral at the margin, while corrected D2's teaching artifact is 'spec + examples': the examples half is elastic, and (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED) a prior-matching surface needs fewer in-context examples and fewer repair iterations to reach a target generation accuracy, because the model's Rust/C generation machinery transfers instead of being rebuilt from spec text. The already-backlogged syntax A/B experiment names this exact quantity — 'the training-distribution penalty decay with in-context examples' — so my central claim is the carded experiment's measurand, not a new speculation. Second, the core's semantics are Rust-class BY DECREE, not coincidence: D1 names Rust-class enforcement as the reference bar, and the floors are Rust-shaped — F001 &mut exclusivity, C004 Send/Sync capabilities (CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02), D001 monomorphization (adversarially CONFIRMED 2026-07-02), Result+trap, and zero implicit conversions (Rust's convention, pointedly NOT C's). For this core, the prior's predictions about what code MEANS are simply correct; an alien surface discards a correct prior and forces reconstruction of construct-to-meaning mappings from in-context text alone — pure loss exactly where the prior was right. Third, error-class dominance on the debate's own terms: round2-syntax names two competing untested silent-error classes — (a) transcription-distance operand-order/associativity swaps when familiar formulas are re-encoded into an alien form, and (b) precedence misbinding under a misremembered precedence table. The familiar-lexeme canonical form keeps tree-isomorphic, fully-parenthesized structure (no precedence table exists, per the floor — killing (b) by construction) while retaining familiar operator atoms and operand order (removing the re-encoding step that generates (a)). The alien form kills only (b) and maximizes (a). Familiar-lexeme + tree-isomorphic grammar weakly dominates on both named classes (both rates EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED; the differential-testing arm decides). Fourth, the canonical-bytes floor is cheapest to satisfy when the mandated bytes coincide with the de facto normal form of the training corpus (rustfmt-style); an alien byte discipline turns every emission into an unpracticed formatting-compliance task. Fifth, precedent: the verification-first surfaces that actually shipped chose restricted-familiar over alien-regular — SPARK is an Ada subset (F006), MISRA-C a C subset; restriction, not alienation, is how regular surfaces have historically been obtained (interpretive design lesson from a semantics card; human-writer precedent, AI transfer untested). Finally, the guard that makes this safe: where semantics diverge, familiarity is actively dangerous — bare '+' must not exist because per-operation numeric modes (N001/N002) have no prior, and C-style promotions (D010) must find no foothold — so every delta is spelled novel. The prior-alignment invariant converts the prior from hazard into a reliable channel ('familiar token implies familiar meaning'), and the verification spine (F003's lesson: no unchecked writer facts) guarantees contamination surfaces as machine-diagnosed compile errors, never miscompiles; the residual hazard is confined to the typechecks-but-wrong class, which the invariant plus the delta-audit directly target. Net: under corrected D2 the real objective is CONDITIONAL description length — the size of the spec+examples artifact needed given the model's prior. Alien-regular minimizes the unconditional spec; familiarity-first minimizes the conditional artifact. The model conditions on its prior whether we exploit it or not.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Rust's &mut exclusivity is the confirmed semantic model D1 adopts as its reference bar; because our ownership semantics genuinely match the Rust prior, Rust-surface conventions for borrows/ownership are semantically truthful, satisfying the position's 'reuse only where semantics match' condition.

Send/Sync-class capability predicates (CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02, scope: data races prevented, general race conditions not) are a required core kernel component; they are Rust machinery by construction, so familiar Rust-style capability surface matches checked semantics rather than costuming alien ones.

Monomorphized generics (adversarially CONFIRMED 2026-07-02) are Rust's model; explicit instantiation has a direct familiar analogue (turbofish-style explicit type arguments), so the mono-only floor can wear a familiar spelling with zero semantic mismatch.

Implicit conversions perturb dispatch/specialization — this is where C-family familiarity would actively mislead (C promotions); it grounds the position's asymmetry: Rust-adjacent (which has zero implicit numeric conversions), not C-adjacent, and novel spellings wherever the prior expects a coercion.

Numeric semantics are per-operation optimizer contracts; bare '+' carries no mode in any prior language, so arithmetic is a designated DELTA: the familiar operator atom may appear only mode-qualified, never bare — the prior-alignment invariant applied.

Named truthful modes per arithmetic node are decided law with no training-distribution precedent; supports mandating a visibly novel mode-qualifier surface at exactly this point while keeping the familiar expression skeleton.

Unchecked writer promises are a miscompilation channel, hence the spine checks every fact; this is why familiar-surface contamination (model autocompletes Rust-isms) degrades to loud machine-diagnosed compile errors rather than unsoundness — bounding the familiarity hazard to the typechecks-but-wrong class.

SPARK is the carded precedent that verification-first design restricts surface expressiveness — and SPARK did so as an Ada SUBSET, i.e., restricted-familiar rather than alien-regular; interpretive design-lesson use of a semantics card, flagged as such, human-writer precedent only.

concessions: The one genuine D2 cost: a Rust-shaped grammar has more node shapes than a uniform S-expression wrapper, so its grammar section of the spec is larger and its parser less trivial. I argue the delta is bounded — the construct inventory is fixed by the round-2 core regardless, each shape is stated once with no alternatives and no precedence — but this rule-count-vs-example-count trade is the honest core of the debate and it is completely unmeasured.

Every benefit I claim from prior transfer is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (zero AI-codegen cards, per the round-2 standing flag). Kill condition, pre-committed: if the syntax A/B shows the training-distribution penalty decays to near-zero within a handful of in-context examples, familiarity's entire benefit column collapses and the alien form's smaller grammar wins outright.

The near-miss/autocomplete hazard is real and is the strongest argument against me: a Rust-adjacent surface invites emission of Rust constructs that do not exist here (?, |x| closures, impl/dyn, inference-reliant elision, mut-default habits). An alien surface forces the model off autopilot. Loud failures are cheap under the decided machine-diagnostics/edit-list architecture, but the mitigation for SILENT failures is only as good as the completeness of the delta audit.

Familiarity helps only the skeleton: the genuinely new vocabulary (effect rows, numeric modes, storage/lifetime contracts, capability predicates, provenance records) is out-of-prior under ANY surface choice, so the benefit is bounded by the skeleton's share of program tokens — and under uniform mandatory annotation that share may be modest.

Normative anchoring ('as in Rust') is NOT admissible spec compression: Rust's prior is partly informal (Nomicon is explanatory, not a formal model — caveats.md), Rust drifts by edition, and the spec must stay self-contained. Familiarity buys example-efficiency and error-rate reduction, never rule elision; every rule is still stated once, in full.

Familiarity is model-generation-relative: it holds for current training corpora, decays or shifts as models retrain, and inverts once a corpus of OUR language exists. The alien-regular position's value is time-invariant; mine is indexed to the present model population.
risks: Silent semantic capture: the model applies the prior's semantics where ours differ and the result typechecks — exactly the invisible-to-verifier class round2-syntax flagged. A single missed entry in the delta audit becomes a standing generation trap; this is the position's dominant failure mode.

Sugar re-entry pressure: 'Rust has it' becomes a recurring argument for ?, method-call syntax, inference, and operator sugar — a governance risk aimed directly at the decided floors. Familiarity-first must be enforced as lexeme-choice-only under the existing toolchain-boundary policing rules, or it becomes a Trojan horse for the cut list.
Delta-audit process burden: every future construct decision needs a prior-alignment review (does any familiar spelling mislead here?); the audit is unowned today and each miss converts into risk 1.

Fuzzy referent: 'semantics match the prior' is judged against Rust-as-trained-into-models, not Rust-as-specified — an informal, version-drifting referent (Nomicon caveat; editions), so match judgments carry irreducible uncertainty at the margins.

Mixed-dialect uncanny valley: a surface that is 80% Rust and 20% novel could plausibly confuse more than either pure form (model oscillates between dialects, applying Rust completions inside novel constructs and vice versa) — untested, and specifically what the three-arm experiment must probe.
Wasted spec mass: if penalty decay is fast (kill condition above), we paid permanent grammar complexity and delta-audit overhead for a transient benefit.
empiricalUntested: Prior transfer reduces examples-to-threshold and first-parse failure for a familiar-lexeme canonical grammar vs an alien regular grammar. Experiment: extend the backlogged syntax A/B (missing-research-backlog.jsonl, round2_empirical 'Syntax A/B') from two arms to three — (i) alien canonical (S-expr-like), (ii) familiar-lexeme canonical (Rust-adjacent, tree-isomorphic, fully parenthesized, mode-qualified atoms), (iii) infix codec — real BPE tokenizers, whole programs; measure generation tokens, first-parse success, examples-to-accuracy-threshold, and penalty-decay slope with pre-committed kill thresholds.

Transcription-distance error rate (silent operand-order/associativity swaps that typecheck) when models re-encode familiar formulas into alien prefix form, vs retention under familiar atoms — round2-syntax hypothesis (a) vs (b); differential-testing arm of the same three-arm experiment.

Rust-autocomplete contamination rate: frequency of emitting nonexistent constructs (?, closures, dyn/impl, elision) under familiar vs alien surface, and repair-loop iterations per contamination class given machine-readable diagnostics. New experiment; decides whether loud-failure cost is as cheap as I claim.

Silent-semantic-capture rate — the direct test of the prior-alignment invariant: seed delta constructs (mode-carrying arithmetic, storage contracts, foreign-shared fields) spelled familiar-with-different-meaning vs visibly-novel; measure typechecks-but-wrong rates under each.

Near-distribution claim: that fully-elaborated, mandatory-annotation Rust-shaped text is measurably closer to the training distribution (perplexity, defect rate) than an alien uniform grammar on matched programs — the structural plausibility ('models have seen annotated/expanded Rust') is not evidence.
Canonical-bytes compliance: formatting-violation rate when the single legal byte formatting is rustfmt-like vs a novel discipline, in a simulated repair loop.
Mixed-dialect (uncanny valley) effect: whether an 80/20 familiar/novel surface yields higher confusion than either pure surface; gradient arm of the three-arm experiment.

Durability: how fast the familiarity advantage shifts across model generations and once in-language corpus exists — a standing monitoring item, not a one-shot experiment; bounds how long the position's benefit column remains valid.

result.position: YES to a hard description-length budget — but scoped to a small mandatory kernel spec only. Architect the spec as a tiny always-in-context kernel (types, ownership triad, calls, mono-generic instantiation, exhaustive match, loops, Result, safe default numeric modes) plus on-demand layer specs (full numeric-mode vocabulary, FFI declaration frames, storage/region contracts, gated-family constructs, concurrency capabilities) served per task by the toolchain. The hard budget binds the kernel; layers get soft budgets and one uniform extension mechanism; the language is architected — and census-gated — so most functions need only kernel constructs.
result.coreArgument: 1) A GLOBAL hard budget is impossible-or-toothless under decided law; only kernel scoping makes 'hard' do design work. The round-2 floors and round-3 safety envelope already commit irreducible description mass: the truthful numeric-mode vocabulary (N001/N002), mandatory FFI declaration frames, storage-contract fields including foreign-shared, the gated family's obligation ledger, capability predicates (C004), operation tables, env-struct encoding. A hard budget over the whole spec must either cut decided floors (illegal — they are owner law per decision-gates.md), under-specify them (pure loop-economics loss), or be set so large it forces nothing. Scoping the hard number to a mandatory kernel is the only shape in which the budget binds live design choices instead of fighting settled ones: every proposed kernel construct is priced against a fixed quota, which operationalizes corrected-D2's actual claim — irregularity, not verbosity, eats the budget — as an enforceable gate. This mirrors the project's own governance pattern everywhere else: trust became a gated, quota-logged ledger (round3-synthesis); contract edits became privileged operations; checks became density-measured. Spec mass is the one resource D2 names first-class; leaving it ungated would be the anomaly. 2) Layered, toolchain-served context is the project's established architecture, not an invention of this position. Compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views on demand (round2-modules line 30; round2-synthesis line 59), the mandatory elaborated re-read artifact (round2-synthesis line 67), and round-3's Layer 0–4 envelope all already treat writer context as a task-scoped, toolchain-mediated artifact family; round3-synthesis line 41 records that the composite deliberately pushes weight toward compiler-elaborated artifacts and toolchain compute to spare the budgets D2 made first-class. The layered spec is the same move applied to the spec itself: kernel always resident; layer specs content-addressed, versioned, retrieved per task. Carded precedent that languages stratify exactly this way: F006 (SPARK confines most code to a restricted fragment and gains verification), A006 (Halide separates a small algorithm core from a schedule layer — and round-2 metaprogramming already binds any schedule layer to declarative in-language structure, i.e., layerable), A007 (target/hardware vocabulary routed through target-parameter constants), F009 (performance machinery preserved below source, off the writer surface). 3) The kernel budget FORCES a specific architecture — this is the payload. (a) Conservative-extension law: a layer may only ADD vocabulary at kernel-defined extension points (annotation slots, mode-name positions, declaration-frame heads); no layer may change the meaning of any kernel-only program; layers may not reference each other — any interaction that cannot factor through kernel extension points must be paid into the kernel or redesigned. This keeps total spec mass linear in layer count rather than quadratic, and it is mechanically checkable over the canonical grammar. (b) Forced partition of decided vocabularies into kernel-common vs layer-exotic: round-3's overflow ruling (unflagged wrap is a legal sound fallback, unproven nsw forbidden) hands the kernel a safe default arithmetic so the full N001/N002 mode ladder lives in the numerics layer without blocking ordinary code; owned/&/&mut is kernel, region/arena and foreign-shared contract fields are layer; match is kernel, operation tables are a layer over the same closed-world sums. (c) Self-announcing constructs: canonical one-spelling form (decided law) makes layer membership a pure syntactic function of the artifact, so READ-side provisioning is deterministic — tooling sees a layer construct and serves its spec; only WRITE-side provisioning is genuinely open (flagged below). (d) Failure containment comes free from decided law: unknown constructs are hard errors under canonical-form-only + closed world, and D1-critical facts are proven-else-checked in ALL build modes (round-3 floor), so an under-provisioned writer costs repair iterations, never soundness — D009 is the carded warning of the alternative (silent degradation under context-dependent interpretation), and the conservative-extension law bans exactly that failure shape. The safety envelope is what makes a hard budget SAFE to run at all: spec compression can never become a correctness hole, only an economics cost, which is the admissible D0 channel. (e) The '80% subset' becomes a measured admission gate, not a hope: a workload census (share of functions parseable under kernel+zero layers) with pre-committed thresholds, exactly the methodology of the metaprogramming comptime census and round-3 frontier sizing. (f) Kernel growth is gated like contract edits — a privileged, budget-priced, audited operation — so the kernel cannot accrete the way round-2's 'periphery' tension warns. (g) Spec provisioning enters provenance: which kernel/layer versions the writer saw is recorded per generation, extending round-3's artifact-honesty invariant to generation-defect postmortems. 4) The owner's verbose-program ruling actively strengthens layering: uniform mandatory annotation means code is substantially self-describing at the site — a reader holding only the kernel spec can navigate code containing layer constructs because the facts are spelled inline rather than implied by distant rules (EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED, flagged). And D2's ban on inference/elision/context-dependence is precisely the property that makes small per-layer specs sufficient: no construct's meaning depends on context a layer spec didn't state once. The alternatives lose on their own terms: a whole-spec hard budget cannot bind without cutting owner law; 'no budget, just discipline' is the aspirational regime the project has rejected for every other scarce resource; a monolithic small spec pretends the decided envelope's mass doesn't exist. Kernel-scoped hard budget + conservatively-extending layers is the only position consistent with both D2-as-corrected and the standing floors.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: SPARK is carded precedent that a language can confine most code to a restricted fragment and gain verification power by doing so — direct support for the viability of a small kernel carrying the 80% path while exotic capability lives outside it.

Halide's algorithm/schedule separation is carded precedent for stratifying a language into a small core plus a separable performance layer; round-2 metaprogramming already binds any schedule layer to declarative in-language structure, i.e., exactly the shape of an on-demand layer spec.

ISPC's SPMD/uniform-varying and target-parameter vocabulary shows hardware-specific vocabulary is naturally separable; the metaprogramming ruling already routes target parameters through the constant sublanguage — layer material, not kernel mass.

Monomorphization-only generics with explicit instantiation is a single uniform expansion rule, stateable once — it keeps the kernel construct set small and regular while retaining expressiveness, making a genuinely tiny kernel plausible.

The UB/poison/undef/freeze ladder shows the numeric contract's true description mass; keeping that always in context for the majority of functions that never touch it is wasted kernel budget — the paradigm case for a mandatory-retrieval layer.

LLVM's separation of default modulo arithmetic from opt-in nsw/nuw promises, combined with round-3's ruling that unflagged wrap is a legal sound fallback, supplies the kernel-safe default mode that lets the full truthful-mode vocabulary live in a layer without blocking ordinary code.

Julia's silent performance/semantic degradation under abstract, context-dependent typing is the carded failure mode the conservative-extension law bans: absence of a layer spec must produce deterministic hard rejection, never silent reinterpretation of look-alike code.

Checker-enforced data-race freedom (with F001-class ownership, both CONFIRMED 2026-07-02) is the backstop that makes spec under-provisioning an iteration-economics problem rather than a soundness problem — the precondition for daring to run any hard budget.

Codegen units as build artifacts rather than semantics is the carded pattern for the serving side: layer packaging is toolchain implementation over one uniform rulebook, adding zero semantic weight — the spec analogue of content-addressed incremental compilation.

Swift SIL preserving source semantics below the surface long enough to optimize supports optimizer-owned interior representation (round-2 already rejected per-binding layout mandates), reducing the writer-facing exotic vocabulary most functions need — load-bearing for the 80%-kernel architecture claim.

concessions: No evidence-based budget NUMBER exists today: committing to a specific token count now would be arbitrary. The mechanism (kernel scoping, gating, conservative-extension layering) can be adopted now; the number must be calibrated by the spec-size ablation and spec-learning-curve experiments. Until then the hard budget is provisional-with-a-placeholder, and I concede an opponent who says 'the number is theater until U5 runs' is right about the number, though not about the mechanism.

This position abandons a hard bound on TOTAL spec size. Layers are a pressure valve, and D2's problem can recur there — 'budget laundering' by keeping the kernel small while dumping mass into layers. Per-layer soft budgets, a layer registry, and census-gated layer admission mitigate but do not eliminate this; a monolith-budget advocate is correct that my hard guarantee is narrower than theirs claims to be.

The clean-partition claim is asserted, not demonstrated. Some cross-cutting vocabularies (numeric modes x generic instantiation, ownership x FFI marshalling, storage contracts x concurrency capabilities) may not factor through kernel extension points; forcing them kernel-ward could inflate the kernel past any meaningful budget. An actual partition draft of the decided floors into kernel/layers is owed before this position hardens into spec.

For D0-hot code the benefit is weakest: performance-critical functions are plausibly layer-dense (modes, storage contracts, schedule structure), so the harness retrieves most layers there anyway. The 80% claim is about function counts, not about the functions that matter most for extreme performance; the layered spec chiefly cheapens the long tail of cold code.

Write-side layer provisioning is a new tooling failure surface with real loop-economics costs (the same admissible cost channel round-2 knowingly accepted for trap=abort's multi-process harness). Read-side determinism via canonical form does not transfer to generation, where the code does not yet exist.

Pre-committed reopening condition: if the spec-size ablation shows current models tolerate the full monolithic spec with no measurable fidelity loss at realistic context sizes, layering is serving complexity for nothing and this verdict should be reopened toward a single spec with the budget as a regularity metric only.
risks: Layer proliferation reproduces round-2's 'periphery accretes into a second evidence-free language' tension at the spec level: each new capability arrives as 'just one more layer'. Mitigation must be structural — a layer registry, per-layer budgets, and the same gated-authorship discipline as contract edits — or the kernel budget becomes a fig leaf.

Avoidance behavior: an under-provisioned writer may emit well-typed kernel-only workarounds instead of the correct layer construct (copying instead of declaring a storage contract; wrap arithmetic where a checked mode was wanted). This type-checks, performs badly, and is invisible to the D1 checker — the well-typed-but-wrong class. Needs lint-class detection analogous to the region-quality/reclamation-timeliness diagnostics, plus measurement in the experiment battery.

Cross-language interference: precisely because the kernel spec is short, models may import Rust/C++/Swift semantics into look-alike kernel constructs (the ownership triad is the obvious target). Kernel examples must adversarially target interference points; whether that works is untested.

Budget gaming: a fixed number invites encoding normative rules as 'examples' or moving them into layer 'commentary'. The budget metric must count everything normative — grammar, rules, annotation vocabulary, AND examples (D2 names all four) — measured with real model tokenizers, per the syntax round's regex-proxy lesson.

Spec/toolchain version skew across kernel + layers misleads the writer silently; kernel and layer specs must be content-addressed and version-pinned like every other artifact in the architecture, and the provisioned set recorded in provenance.

Retrieval timing: mid-task layer-spec injection may dilute attention or arrive after the model has already committed to a wrong construct shape; if only up-front provisioning works, the harness must over-provision, eroding the kernel's context advantage.
empiricalUntested: U1 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: a smaller always-resident kernel spec yields higher generation fidelity per context token than a full monolithic spec. Experiment: spec-size ablation — identical task battery under kernel-only / kernel+needed-layers / full monolith; measure first-parse success, silent semantic-error rate via differential testing, and repair iterations; real BPE tokenizers on whole programs (extends the round-2 syntax A/B protocol).

U2 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: kernel-only writers do not hallucinate invented layer-construct spellings at a loop-breaking rate, given deterministic hard rejection. Experiment: temptation tasks that invite layer constructs under kernel-only context; measure hallucinated-construct rate, avoidance-workaround rate, and iterations-to-green.

U3 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: write-side layer-need detection is automatable — the harness can predict the layer set from a task description before code exists. Experiment: task→layer-set prediction accuracy; under/over-provisioning rates and their loop-latency costs.

U4 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (census, corpus does not exist): most functions in representative AI-generated performance-critical code need only kernel constructs. Experiment: workload census over generated corpora — share of functions parseable under kernel+zero layers — with pre-committed admission/reopen thresholds, mirroring the metaprogramming comptime census and round-3 frontier sizing.

U5 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (calibrates the budget number and the entire D2 operating point): how in-context acquisition error scales with spec size and example count for a genuinely novel language. Experiment: spec+examples in-context across graduated spec sizes vs a fine-tuned baseline; the corpus has zero AI-codegen cards, so even the premise that in-context spec learning reaches needed fidelity at ANY size is uncarded.

U6 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: uniform verbose annotation makes code self-describing enough that a kernel-spec-only reader acts correctly around layer constructs it cannot emit. Experiment: read-modify tasks on layer-dense code with and without layer specs provisioned; measure edit correctness and repair iterations.

U7 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: mid-task retrieval of layer specs performs as well as up-front provisioning. Experiment: same tasks with interleaved vs preloaded layer specs; measure fidelity, latency, and context-growth costs.

U8 EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: conservative-extension layering actually prevents the D009-class failure in practice — i.e., models never silently reinterpret kernel programs after seeing layer specs. Experiment: differential generation/reading of identical kernel programs with varying layer sets provisioned; any semantic divergence falsifies the layering's central safety claim.

result.position: Curriculum as build artifact: the toolchain deterministically generates, budgets, verifies, and CI-gates the teaching pack — kernel spec + N task-shaped canonical examples auto-extracted from the verified codebase + the closed diagnostics vocabulary — regenerated on every language change, with model generation benchmarks against the regenerated pack as a shipping gate. Teaching quality is a tested invariant, not a document.
result.coreArgument:
The debate question — how much teaching burden moves from the in-context spec to the interactive repair loop — cannot be answered as doctrine without violating the project's own standing rule: the corpus contains zero AI-codegen evidence, so every rival position commits, permanently, to an unmeasured model-behavior claim. My position is the only one whose central mechanism converts that claim into a continuously measured quantity with pre-committed thresholds — the exact epistemic move this project already uses everywhere else (ordered measurement programs, reopen thresholds, gate-before-promote). The split between spec-teaching and loop-teaching becomes a tunable knob read off a benchmark, not a debate verdict.

(1) The pack makes corrected D2 ENFORCEABLE. D2 constrains spec compactness: few rules, uniform, each stated once; what eats the budget is irregularity. A prose spec cannot police that — compactness of a document is a judgment call. A generated pack sized to a token budget can: every proposed sugar, special case, or inference rule shows up as measurable pack growth plus a benchmark pass-rate delta, so a D2 violation fails CI instead of being litigated. The teaching pack is D2's regression test.

(2) It is the decided architecture applied to teaching, not a new invention. Round-2 already mandates: machine-readable node-path diagnostics as a universal contract; the compiler-emitted elaborated artifact as the mandatory re-read form; compiler-GENERATED token-compact interface views (never stored); site-enumerated mechanical edit lists; deterministic external generators under provenance checking (consistency mechanism 2); content-addressed caching on canonical bytes (D001/D004 channel). The teaching pack is one more generated, provenance-checked, content-addressed view over the same canonical substrate — regenerated deterministically, diffed on regeneration, never a second stored spelling, obeying round2-syntax's toolchain-boundary policing. Crucially, the canonical-form floor makes example extraction SOUND: with exactly one byte-level spelling per construct, every extracted example is a re-verified, content-addressed subtree of the shipping codebase. The curriculum literally cannot drift out of sync with the language — the rot that kills hand-written docs and hand-picked examples is structurally excluded, the same way mechanism 2 excludes generated-code drift.

(3) What the toolchain must guarantee (the topic's second half): the repair loop is a teaching channel only if lessons are deterministic and reusable. Concretely: (a) a CLOSED, versioned diagnostics vocabulary — every diagnostic class enumerated in the pack with one canonical repair example; novel free-text errors are toolchain bugs; (b) the already-decided single-hop repair-informativeness floors (closures diagnostics naming all three artifacts; dispatch edit lists; template-level aggregation to one repair site); (c) byte-stable determinism — same input, same diagnostic (canonical form + deterministic checker provide this); (d) all compiler output echoes canonical form, so every repair iteration doubles as a worked example; (e) machine-readable trap-with-report for the runtime half (C002, the only carded post-violation observability precedent). Under these guarantees a diagnostic is a just-in-time lesson that fires exactly when the model's prior misfires.

(4) The split this yields: uniform rules stay in the spec because D2 makes them spec-cheap (stated once). What moves to the repair loop is PRIOR-CORRECTION — the long tail of places where a model's C-family/Rust priors contradict this language. Teaching those preemptively is exactly the special-case text D2 bans from the spec; teaching them lazily via deterministic diagnostics costs tokens only when a prior actually misfires. Spec carries the rule kernel; extracted examples carry the idiom layer; diagnostics carry the anti-prior curriculum. The per-rule equilibrium is then discovered by ablation under a fixed budget (drop rule text, add repair example, measure first-shot errors and iterations-to-green against pre-committed thresholds) — not asserted.

(5) The CI gate closes the record's biggest process hole. Rounds 2-3 keep amending floors and multiplying report families (check reports, lifetime reports, discharge ledger, wall-crossing logs); each amendment silently changes what a writer must know. Without a gate, teachability degrades invisibly until production failure. With it, any change that makes the language less writable by the actual writer population fails before shipping — extending N006/Alive2 gate-before-ship discipline from semantics preservation to writability, and instantiating J001/J002's structural lesson that systems speculating on an assumption carry guard-and-invalidation machinery. Under D0, 'models can still write this language' is as load-bearing as 'this optimization is sound'; it deserves the same class of gate. The cost lands on the admissible channels: toolchain compute and build latency (D001/D004; round3-synthesis line 41 records this bias as the D2-conformant default), never on spec budget or safety floors — the pack teaches the envelope, it cannot touch it.

citedEvidence.howItSupports: Monomorphization's carded compile-time/code-size cost channel establishes toolchain compute as an admissible spend (round2-syntax line 28 already uses D001 to justify content-addressed caching as an iteration-loop win); pack regeneration and benchmark inference spend the same admissible channel under D0.

Codegen-units/LTO evidence that build partitioning trades compile time against optimization makes the build loop a first-class, priceable design surface — which is exactly where the teaching-quality CI gate lives.

SPARK is carded precedent that restricting surface expressiveness keeps the source-to-rules mapping small and checkable; the round-2 canonical-form/no-sugar floor gives the same property, which is what makes a budgeted kernel spec plus sound automatic example extraction feasible at all.

InstCombine's canonicalization discipline with proof-grade (Alive2) validation expectations is precedent for gating changes on validation before shipping; the teaching benchmark extends gate-before-ship from semantics preservation to writability.

Go's halt-on-detected-race is the only carded post-violation observability precedent; it underwrites machine-readable trap-with-report as the runtime half of the closed diagnostics vocabulary the pack must enumerate and teach.

(CONFIRMED_WITH_AMENDED_SOURCES 2026-07-02, scope: data races prevented, general race conditions not) — the type-system route makes D1-class failures compile-time checker diagnostics: deterministic, complete lessons the repair loop can teach, instead of nondeterministic runtime evidence it cannot.

Structural precedent, not direct evidence: speculative specialization requires guard/invalidation machinery when assumptions break; the benchmark gate is the guard for the teaching assumption ('models can write this language'), invalidating (blocking) changes when it fails.

Halide's algorithm/schedule separation with search in tooling is precedent for placing search in the toolchain loop rather than the language; pack-composition search (which rules ride the spec vs which move to repair examples) is the same move, matching round-2's metaprogramming replacement (schedule SEARCH in the AI/tooling loop).

The canonical single-spelling floor makes example extraction dialect-drift-free; 'What must be true' lines 24-28 already mandate drift surfacing, node-path diagnostics, and content-addressed caching; follow-up line 38 (training-distribution penalty decay with in-context examples) names the exact unmeasured quantity my gate operationalizes as a permanent measurement.

Lines 66-76 (machine-readable diagnostics as universal contract, mandatory elaborated re-read artifact, site-enumerated edit lists, generator/provenance infrastructure) are the decided mechanisms the pack reuses; the pack generator is a mechanism-2 deterministic generator whose source-of-record output is the curriculum; line 104 already demands the repair-loop asymmetry experiment my position institutionalizes.

The artifact-honesty invariant and report families define the diagnostics vocabulary that must be taught; line 41 records the D2-favorable push toward toolchain compute (the pack spends exactly that channel, and line 41 flags it unmeasured); line 42's acceptance-stability tension is the precedent I concede my model-relative gate re-instantiates.

Trap-with-report=abort plus the multi-process harness wall-clock question ('What must be true' line 26) is the accepted-but-unmeasured loop-economics cost my repair-loop-leaning split compounds with — grounding the joint iterations-to-green x harness-wall-clock experiment.

concessions: The foundational claim — that models can acquire a novel canonical-form language from a budgeted in-context pack at production-grade pass rates at all — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED (zero AI-codegen cards). If in-context acquisition fundamentally fails, the pack is a well-engineered curriculum for a student who cannot learn, and no CI gate fixes that; the position degrades to 'we find out early and cheaply', which is weaker than 'it works'.

The gate is process-relative: pass rates depend on model version, sampling regime, and harness. This re-instantiates the round-3 acceptance-stability tension (line 42) in the teaching domain. Pinned model versions, content-addressed packs, and artifact-recorded benchmark runs narrow but do not eliminate it — a gate passed under model A certifies nothing carded about model B.

Bootstrap circularity: early packs extract examples from a small codebase written by models taught by the previous pack. Frequency-weighted extraction can self-fulfillingly exclude constructs (never used, so never taught, so never used). The generator therefore needs coverage-forcing (exhaustive grammar and diagnostics-vocabulary coverage), which dilutes the purity of 'examples are real verified code' for rare constructs.

Goodhart risk is real: co-optimizing language and pack against benchmark tasks can diverge from real optimizer-workload quality. Benchmark validity itself needs a held-out transfer study; until that exists, the gate certifies pack-passing, not production writability.

The pack generator, task extractor, and benchmark harness are new trusted tooling — a 'teaching TCB'. It cannot corrupt D1 (the checker still rejects bad programs regardless of what was taught) but it can silently teach falsehoods, and the benchmark shares blast radius if the extractor is wrong. Pack-faithfulness validation (every normative pack statement executable as a test against the checker) is a required deliverable this position adds and has not designed.

Gate friction can veto good changes: a beneficial semantics fix that transiently drops pass rate (models anchored on the old pack) gets blocked. The gate must run against the REGENERATED pack, and even then the transition cost decays at an unmeasured rate — exactly the round2-syntax follow-up quantity (in-context penalty decay).

The pack inherits every open design hole — FFI periphery, interior-annotation regime, bare-'+' default, concurrent-trap semantics — and cannot gate on unspecified semantics. Separately, rounds 2-3 keep growing the report/diagnostic families, and 'rich enough for single-hop repair' fights 'small enough to teach' inside one budget; that tension is unpriced.
risks: Benchmark flakiness: sampling nondeterminism makes pass-rate gates statistically noisy; without pre-committed statistical thresholds and variance methodology, the gate either blocks spuriously (eroding trust, inviting overrides) or is loosened until vacuous.

CI cost: real-model inference on every language/toolchain change is expensive and slow; D0 admits the channel, but an over-heavy gate throttles the very iteration speed the content-addressed-caching architecture was built to win.

Compounded loop economics: a repair-loop-heavy teaching split lowers first-shot quality, multiplying iterations-to-green against the already-accepted multi-process trap=abort harness wall-clock cost (round2-synthesis line 72); the two loop costs are multiplicative and neither is measured.

Model-idiom lock-in: gating language evolution on current models' pass rates risks baking today's model priors into permanent language design — the language optimizes toward what 2026 models find easy rather than what the optimizer needs.

Under-teaching of read-only surface: budget pressure incentivizes dropping Layer-2/Layer-3 (gated family, FFI frames) material because steady-state writers cannot author it — but they must still READ and reason about it; a pack that teaches only the writable surface leaves a latent comprehension failure around exactly the trust-critical constructs.

Asynchronous staleness: if the gate fires only on language changes, model-version changes arriving between language releases silently invalidate the certified pack; the gate needs a re-certification trigger on writer-population change, which no current process owns.

Curriculum-quality monoculture: if pack, benchmark, and writer are all derived from the same model family, benchmark success may reflect shared blind spots (correlated-error concern already flagged for same-model review in round-3 residuals) rather than genuine teachability.
empiricalUntested: EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: models acquire the novel canonical language from a budgeted in-context pack at production pass rates. Experiment: pack acquisition benchmark — kernel spec + N extracted examples, task-shaped generation, measuring first-parse success and first-shot semantic correctness as functions of N and budget; extends the round2-syntax follow-up on training-distribution penalty decay with in-context examples.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: pack-benchmark pass rate predicts real-task generation quality. Experiment: held-out transfer study — correlate pack scores with success on fresh tasks never extracted into the pack, pre-committed correlation threshold; failure demotes the gate from shipping-blocker to advisory.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: spec-rule vs repair-loop substitution is viable (the debate's core quantity). Experiment: per-rule ablation — remove rule text from the kernel, add one diagnostic repair example, measure deltas in first-shot error rate and iterations-to-green under a fixed pack budget with pre-committed thresholds; the resulting per-rule table IS the answer to 'how much burden moves'.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: a diagnostic-plus-repair-example generalizes — models fix unseen instances of a taught diagnostic class. Experiment: repair-transfer test per diagnostic class; extends round2-synthesis line 104 (repair-loop asymmetry: fix success and latency on literal-source vs backtrace diagnostics).

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: pack-budget response curve has a usable knee. Experiment: budget titration — pass rate and iterations-to-green vs pack size across task families; locates the budget the position presupposes exists.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: pass rates are stable across model versions/vendors. Experiment: cross-model portability study on a frozen pack; decides gate pinning and re-certification policy, and sizes the process-relativity concession.

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: writers act on lint-class (non-error) feedback in-loop — required for the lint half of the diagnostics vocabulary to carry teaching load (already named at round2-synthesis line 104).

EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED: repair-heavy teaching remains affordable under trap=abort loop economics. Experiment: joint measurement of iterations-to-green (per pack composition) x multi-process harness wall-clock per detected defect (round2-error-handling follow-up), with a pre-committed threshold that, if breached, forces teaching burden back toward the spec side of the knob.

challenges.challenge: The 'approximately spec-mass-neutral at the margin' claim contradicts the position's own concession 1. Where Position 1 differs from Position 3 — Rust-shaped node shapes, brace/arrow structure, statement-like grammar forms rather than one uniform wrapper — is exactly where D2 charges rent (more productions = more rules, even with one spelling each and no precedence). The part of the position that is genuinely spec-neutral (token strings) collapses into Position 3; the part that is distinctively Position 1 (grammar shape inventory) is the D2-priced part. The position cannot claim neutrality for the whole while conceding cost for its distinguishing half.

'Weakly dominates on both named classes' is an empirical claim dressed as structural. Round2-syntax's class (a) is transcription-distance error when re-encoding familiar formulas. A fully parenthesized, tree-isomorphic, mode-qualified form — even with familiar operator atoms — is still a re-encoding away from the infix the model would write unprompted: mandatory parenthesization and per-node mode qualifiers are themselves transcription steps that can introduce silent operand-order/nesting errors. Familiar atoms REDUCE distance; they do not remove the re-encoding step. Dominance on class (a) is therefore untested, not established by construction.

'The prior's predictions about what code MEANS are simply correct' for the matched region is too strong on the record's own evidence. C004's amended scope (2026-07-02) is the corpus's demonstration that Rust-shaped intuitions overclaim even about Rust (data races prevented; general race conditions not). And the model's prior operates on code SHAPES — methods, ?, closures, inference, elision — all of which are cut here; the genuinely matched region is rule-level (ownership exclusivity, mono generics), while shape-level predictions are wrong almost everywhere under uniform mandatory annotation. The claimed transfer channel is narrower than the argument's rhetoric.

The claim that 'my central claim is the carded experiment's measurand' over-launders novelty through the backlog. The backlogged Syntax A/B (missing-research-backlog.jsonl line 43) is a TWO-arm canonical-vs-infix experiment; it names penalty-decay as a measurand for that pair. The familiar-lexeme-canonical arm — the arm Position 1's entire case rides on — exists in no backlog item or card. Extending to three arms is a new, unfiled proposal, and pre-committed kill thresholds for it exist nowhere.

The prior-alignment invariant is not one-line D2-cheap in practice. As spec TEXT it is one sentence, but its enforcement is N per-construct match judgments against 'Rust-as-trained-into-models' — an informal, edition-drifting, partly Nomicon-documented referent (caveats.md) — plus a standing delta audit the position itself admits is unowned. D2 counts irregularity; a surface whose familiar/novel boundary is decided case-by-case against a fuzzy external referent is a governance-level special-case generator even if each outcome is stated once.

Citation discipline: D010 is cited as support without the required unverified flag. D010 is direct-extraction, absent from the 2026-07-02 confirmation log, and named on round3-synthesis line 78's list of cards 'that still carry no votes'; round2-type-inference line 31 mandates the caveat travel with D/A/C/J citations.

The loud/silent asymmetry — the position's decisive third argument — is contradicted by the record it cites. Round2-syntax names a silent, typechecks-and-verifier-invisible error class SPECIFIC to the alien form: transcription-distance operand-order/associativity swaps ('errors that type-check and are invisible to the verifier'). 'A regular-alien surface fails LOUD' is false as stated; both surfaces own a record-named silent class (alien: re-encoding swaps; familiar: prior-import token selection), so the detectability asymmetry is not architectural — the question is which unmeasured silent rate is larger, which is exactly the empirical question the position claims to bypass.

The flagship exhibit — a Rust-looking '+' that 'lowers to a different per-operation contract' — is already unrepresentable under decided law: the round-2 NUMERICS floor mandates each arithmetic node names exactly one mode, round-3 forbids unproven nsw emission, and every advocate (including Position 1) bans bare '+'. The position's own concession 3 admits the mandatory-explicit-facts floor is the load-bearing defense and alienness only 'second-order reinforcement' — which concedes that the headline hazard is mostly defused by floors that hold under EVERY surface choice, leaving the increment alienness buys unmeasured and probably small.

Substantial strawman burden: argument (1) prices a 13+-level precedence table, statement/expression duality, sugar, and elision, and argument (4) prices multiple byte forms per tree — but Position 1 explicitly forswears all of these (single canonical bytes, no precedence, no sugar, no elision, operating inside the round-2 floors), and the claim that familiarity-first 'would reopen a closed verdict' is wrong for the same reason: round2-syntax decided canonical-form-only and A001's negative scope leaves lexeme/shape choice undecided. After Position 2's own steelman point (5) admits familiar lexemes, its live disagreement with Position 3 is nearly nil and with Position 1 is node-shape inventory only — far narrower than the argument's rhetoric.

'Half this debate is decidable by arithmetic' overreaches under the position's own D2 reading. User-directives line 16 places 'examples' INSIDE the description that must be small; production-counting decides rule mass only, and cannot settle the budget unless the examples-footprint differential is shown second-order — which is precisely the untested behavioral claim (their concession 5 admits this, but the core argument still bills the arithmetic as settling 'the spec-budget half').

Citation discipline: D009 is deployed as 'the canonical silent-failure exhibit' without the required unverified flag — it is direct-extraction, on round3-synthesis line 78's still-unverified list, and round2-type-inference line 31 requires that caveat to travel with it. Also, the accusation that familiarity's 'token-compactness argument is owner-dead' misfires: Position 1 never argues program-token compactness; the only token argument in play is Position 3's spec+examples-footprint argument, which is not the voided program-token reading.

Internal contradiction between leg (c) and concession 3: if spec definitions are fully self-contained (the conceded-safe variant), the one-line 'differs from Rust:' deltas are redundant spec mass serving no checking function; if the deltas are load-bearing for model behavior, the spec's effective semantics are conditioned on an unverified, edition-drifting prior — definition-by-delta by another name, which the concession disclaims. The position must pick one and re-price accordingly.

The showcase example contains an unflagged false friend: Rust's 'unchecked_*' arithmetic is UB-on-overflow, while the corresponding optimizer promise here is nsw/nuw-POISON — and N001's ladder (immediate UB > poison > undef) makes exactly that distinction load-bearing. Borrowing Rust's mode vocabulary 'pre-stabilized' would import a semantic blur at precisely a divergence point, in the very example offered as proof the borrowing is safe. Relatedly, the 'arrives pre-stabilized' gloss on round2-type-inference line 30 is a non sequitur: line 30 demands OUR name-to-semantics mapping stop churning; fixed Rust strings over churning semantics is the position's own 'standing lie' risk, not stability.

The BPE single-token argument is stacked and rests on an interpretive D2 reading presented as settled. Stacked: the alien alternative is assumed to use fragmenting invented atoms, but an alien-regular lexicon can equally choose short single-token ASCII atoms, dissolving the differential. Interpretive: D2's stated spend unit is rule count/irregularity ('few rules, uniform, each stated once'); reading 'must be small' as BPE-token-small for the spec+examples artifact is defensible but not owner-ruled, and the position bills it as 'the one place lexicon choice genuinely touches the D2 budget' without flagging that the unit itself needs an owner call. The adjacent sentence 'program text is saturated with vocabulary tokens' gestures at the voided program-token channel and should be excised or explicitly disclaimed.

unsupportedClaims: P1: 'The model conditions on its prior whether we exploit it or not' — a mechanism claim about in-context learning with zero cards, stated as fact and used to convert the debate's objective into 'conditional description length'; EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED and unnamed as such in the core argument (the experiment that would test it is the spec-consultation/error-category-shift probe both other positions name).

P1: 'Familiar-lexeme + tree-isomorphic grammar weakly dominates on both named classes' — presented as structural dominance; class (a) is reduced, not eliminated, because mandatory full parenthesization and mode qualifiers are themselves re-encoding steps. Both rates are untested; 'weakly dominates' is not derivable by construction.

P1: rustfmt-coincident canonical bytes are 'cheapest to satisfy' / alien byte discipline is 'an unpracticed formatting-compliance task' — asserted in the core argument as a benefit while appearing in the position's own empirical-untested list; no card, no measurement.

P2: 'A regular-alien surface fails LOUD: unfamiliarity surfaces as parse/check errors routed into the repair loop' — directly contradicted by round2-syntax's carded-in-record silent class for the alien form (transcription-distance swaps that type-check and are invisible to the verifier).

P2: 'A perfectly uniform form has the smallest true description because... plus the examples needed to teach it in context' — the rule-count half is analytic; silently extending the minimality claim to include examples-to-teach smuggles the untested examples-efficiency question into an arithmetic-looking statement (their own concession 5 contradicts it).

P2: familiarity's 'ONLY surviving argument is behavioral prior-leverage' — overlooks Position 3's D2-internal argument that the spec's own teaching examples are written in the surface and sit inside the protected budget, which is a spec-budget argument, not a behavioral one.

P3: 'Familiar lexemes (fn, mut, i32, match) are single tokens in current BPE vocabularies; invented atoms fragment into multi-token sequences' — mechanically checkable (their own E3) but unchecked, asserted directionally as fact, and dependent on the strawman that the alien lexicon must use invented fragmenting atoms.

P3: 'for most of the vocabulary the Rust name is the TRUE name' — depends on a divergence census that has never been run; the position's own concession 1 admits a large census flips the D2 accounting, and the wrapping/checked/saturating/unchecked showcase already contains one blurred divergence (UB vs poison).

P1 and P2 jointly: citing D010 (P1) and D009 (P2) as load-bearing support without the direct-extraction/unverified caveat required by round2-type-inference line 31 and round3-synthesis line 78 — a caveats.md standing-rule violation in both briefs.

P1: 'the already-backlogged syntax A/B experiment names this exact quantity' — the penalty-decay measurand is named for canonical-vs-infix arms only; no carded experiment contains a familiar-lexeme-canonical arm, so the central comparison is uncarded, not 'the carded experiment's measurand'.
missingConsiderations: The D2 unit-of-measure question is never posed to the owner: user-directives line 16 puts 'examples' inside the description that must be small, while line 18 defines the spend as irregularity/rule-count. Position 2 wins by definition under a rule-count reading; Positions 1/3 can win only under a total-in-context-teaching-footprint reading. Each brief argues from its preferred unit; none requests the owner ruling that would make the spec-mass arithmetic and the token census commensurable.

Falsifiability of spec self-containedness: with a familiar lexicon, a passing teachability eval can never distinguish spec-driven from prior-driven competence — models cannot be de-trained of Rust — so 'the spec+examples suffice' is untestable on the familiar arm and testable only on the alien arm. This is a first-order experimental-design and long-term-assurance asymmetry (spec bugs masked by prior compensation surface only when the prior shifts) that no position raises.

Skeleton-share census, computable now and cheap: under the round-3 envelope (checks, drops, frames, ledger entries, provenance records as explicit artifact ops) plus uniform mandatory annotation, what fraction of tokens in a representative elaborated artifact belongs to constructs any Rust prior covers at all? P1 concedes the share 'may be modest' but nobody proposes measuring it before pricing the familiarity benefit — it upper-bounds Positions 1 and 3 simultaneously and requires no model in the loop.

The repair loop as an in-context example generator: every diagnostic/edit-list iteration feeds the model more canonical-form text, so effective penalty decay in production may be dominated by session-accumulated exposure rather than curated spec examples. This bears directly on P2's persistent-tax risk and P1's pre-committed kill condition, and no position models it.

Interaction with the open interior-annotation/elision regime and the elaborator-as-codec tension (round2-type-inference; round2-synthesis open tension): if elision wins its experiment, the surface the model WRITES differs from the stored canonical form it RE-READS — familiarity/regularity could be split across the write and read surfaces, and 'which artifact is source of record during repair' is undecided. All three positions implicitly assume one surface serves both directions.

Diagnostics-mediated repair changes what familiarity is worth: node-path machine diagnostics and site-enumerated edit lists make much writer interaction structured-data-driven rather than fluent-code-reading. The marginal value of surface familiarity CONDITIONAL on the decided tooling architecture is a different (unposed) empirical question from raw generation familiarity, and it is the one that actually matters here.

Tokenizer-level symmetry of the single-token property: the same fact that makes 'fn'/'match' cheap tokens (P3's upside) makes Rust continuations mechanically high-probability after them — the token-frequency benefit and the autocomplete-contamination hazard are the same coin, and no position weighs them as such; the contamination experiments test the hazard but no brief connects it to the claimed token benefit.

Ownership and sequencing of the decision procedure: P1 names the delta audit unowned; nobody assigns ownership of the borrow-vs-mint policy, the divergence census, the spec-mass arithmetic P2 wants run first, or the E3 tokenizer census — and no position states which computable-now artifacts must exist BEFORE the model experiments are interpretable (e.g., the census defines where the false-friend battery's traps go).
result.sharpestTension: Corrected D2 fixes WHAT is budgeted but not the UNIT, and the tie-breaker both sides reach for does not break the tie.
