Under a rule-count/irregularity reading (user-directives line 18), Position 2's argmin claim is true by definition and the debate is over before any experiment; under a total in-context teaching-footprint reading (line 16, which places 'examples' inside the description that must be small), Positions 1/3 can win, but only via examples-efficiency and error-rate claims that are 100% EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED with zero AI-codegen cards. Both readings are textually supported and no one has asked the owner to fix the unit. Each side then tries to escape the deadlock through the silent-error asymmetry — and that escape fails symmetrically: the record itself names a verifier-invisible typechecks-but-wrong class for EACH surface (round2-syntax's transcription-distance swaps for the alien form; prior-import/wrong-token selection for the familiar form), while the decided floors (mandatory per-node modes, canonical single-spelling, grammar-rejected unlicensed forms) partially defuse BOTH flagship exhibits. So the residue that actually decides the debate is the relative rate of two silent error classes that no card measures, no backlogged experiment (as filed — the Syntax A/B is two-arm) currently compares, and whose measurement has a shelf life across model generations shorter than the irreversibility of the surface decision it is supposed to settle.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 1 (hard CI-enforced 10-20k whole-spec budget as decision gate)
challenges.challenge: The position's own concessions dissolve its headline. It concedes the 10-20k anchor is un-carded, that the gate is 'hard-in-procedure, not hard-in-threshold' until a floor-pricing audit runs, and that budget-broken ties need reopening conditions tied to pre-empted experiments. What survives those concessions — counted deltas on every change, rule-count as co-equal metric, no-shadow-spec rule, number derived after calibration — is operationally almost identical to Position 3's ratchet-plus-measured-number regime. The debate-relevant residue is only whether an arbitrary anchor is pre-registered before U5-class data exists, and the position's own risk 1 (a moved number permanently damages the gate's credibility) is an argument AGAINST pre-registering a number it admits is likely wrong: full Lua/Scheme manuals plausibly bust 20k by its own admission.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 1 (hard CI-enforced 10-20k whole-spec budget as decision gate)
challenges.challenge: The retrodiction argument (point 2) is asserted, never computed, and one-sided. No pricing of round-2 verdicts in tokens or rules exists anywhere in the corpus. Worse, round 2 did not only cut spec-expensive things — it MANDATED spec-mass-heavy writer-visible machinery: typed operation tables with coherence and construction-coupling rules, capability predicates, the contract-group kernel, FFI declaration frames, the named numeric-mode vocabulary, storage/lifetime contracts on every allocation, env-struct call-interface encoding with five mandated diagnostic classes (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md lines 22-33, 52-62). The gate could just as easily fail to retrodict the KEPT side. Until the floors are actually tokenized, 'the budget reproduces the verdict lattice' is unfalsifiable curve-fitting used as the position's main evidence.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 1 (hard CI-enforced 10-20k whole-spec budget as decision gate)
challenges.challenge: Two card-transfer overclaims do load-bearing work. (a) C004's confirmed scope is data-race prevention via Send/Sync + &mut exclusivity — and the 2026-07-02 amendment explicitly SHARPENED scope to 'data races prevented, general race conditions not'. D1's kernel reserve also includes the full ownership/borrow/lifetime system, the round-3 obligation ledger, gated fact-boundary family, and FFI frames; C004 is not 'direct evidence the D1 kernel reserve plausibly fits inside a Lua/Scheme-class budget'. (b) N006 prices optimizer FOLDS at Alive2 proof-grade cost; spec tokens are not verifier obligations — annotation vocabulary and examples add tokens with zero proof obligations, while one ownership rule induces many. The 'dual-support / fails-safe' argument therefore does not establish TOKENS as the right gated unit even on the carded axis; rule/production count captures the N006/F003 benefit without tokenizer dependence — which collapses most of the carded case into Position 3's regime.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 1 (hard CI-enforced 10-20k whole-spec budget as decision gate)
challenges.challenge: Internal contradiction on tie-breaking: core argument 2 advocates spec-cost as 'the only measurable tie-breaker that exists NOW' for the interfaces/closures/sharing tensions that round 2 ruled 'empirically unarbitrable today' with ordered experiments and pre-committed thresholds; concession 5 then retracts this to 'pre-registered input to their experiments' decision criteria'. These force different actions on the open tensions and the position never says which it holds. Additionally, its asymmetry rhetoric strawmans the opposition: Position 3 does not need a flat spec-size curve — it concedes a deployment outer bound exists and supports a measurement-derived number; it needs only that the knee's location is unknown, which is exactly the uncarded state of the corpus.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 (kernel-scoped hard budget + on-demand layered specs)
challenges.challenge: The layered architecture answers a different constraint than corrected D2 states. D2 binds the language's DESCRIPTION — few rules total, uniform, each stated once. Layering does not shrink total description; it GROWS it (conservative-extension law, layer registry, extension-point definitions, provisioning protocol, per-layer budgets, version-pinning rules are all new normative mass) in exchange for smaller RESIDENT context share. That trade is only rational if resident share, not total description, is the binding variable — which is precisely the position's own untested premises U1/U7. So the position either amends D2 from a compactness constraint into a deployment-serving constraint on uncarded evidence, or it must concede that under D2's literal accounting it makes the spec larger. Its concession that it 'abandons a hard bound on TOTAL spec size' concedes the D2-literal reading loses.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 (kernel-scoped hard budget + on-demand layered specs)
challenges.challenge: The kernel/layer partition collides with round-2 floors in at least two named assignments. (a) The MEMORY floor makes storage/lifetime contracts mandatory on EVERY allocation (round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md line 29), so storage-contract vocabulary is majority-path surface, not exotic layer material — putting it behind retrieval either breaks the 80%-kernel-sufficiency architecture or forces it kernel-ward, inflating the kernel past a meaningful budget. (b) Typed operation tables are 'the blessed data-driven form' of CORE dispatch (line 27), not a peripheral capability. The position concedes the partition is undemonstrated in general, but these are not open questions — they are already-decided floors that contradict the sketch as written, and they should have been caught before the position asserted 'the forced partition of decided vocabularies' as a payload.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 (kernel-scoped hard budget + on-demand layered specs)
challenges.challenge: Citation defects at load-bearing points. (a) 'F001-class ownership, both CONFIRMED 2026-07-02' is false: the 07-02 adversarial verification set is A001/A002/A006/C004/D001/D002/D003/D005/D007/J004 (notes/card-verification-2026-07-02.jsonl); F001's verification is the original deep-research pass. (b) D004 is about codegen units/LTO compile-time-vs-runtime tradeoffs and says nothing about serving or packaging spec layers — analogy dressed as card support. (c) D009 is a still-unverified provisional card (decision-gates.md line 49) whose claim is Julia PERFORMANCE degradation under abstract typing; reading it as evidence about 'silent reinterpretation of look-alike code' under missing layer specs is an uncarded extrapolation propping up the conservative-extension law's central safety analogy. (d) The N001 paradigm case is miscounted: under the round-2 numerics floor the writer-facing spec is the named-mode vocabulary; the UB/poison/undef/freeze ladder is compiler-internal under EITHER architecture, so the flagship 'mass moved to a layer' was never kernel mass.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 2 (kernel-scoped hard budget + on-demand layered specs)
challenges.challenge: The 'impossible-or-toothless' dichotomy against whole-spec budgets attacks a strawman: Position 1's design prices the floors first as a kernel reserve and gates only the remainder — that shape neither cuts owner law nor is toothless, so kernel-scoping needs a better justification than floor-collision. Meanwhile layering introduces a new global irregularity axis D2 taxes: which construct belongs to which layer is itself description mass the writer must hold (or a retrieval dependency), i.e., a context-dependence of exactly the kind corrected D2 enumerates as budget-eating — and the position counts it nowhere.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 3 (regularity invariants + ratchet, no pre-registered number)
challenges.challenge: The ratchet inherits the arbitrariness it condemns, one level down and with weaker failure visibility. Initial per-layer rule-count bounds are judgment numbers chosen with zero acquisition data (conceded); a ratchet binds the DELTA from an unmeasured level and cannot detect that the level is already too high — detection depends entirely on the learning-curve harness the position itself lists as a critical-path risk that, if delayed, forces either stalled spec evolution or a waived gate ('worse than a crude cap', its own words). And the position's own governance-asymmetry risk cuts against itself: caps fail loudly, ratchets fail quietly. In the interim before any experiment runs, the regime it offers is strictly weaker than it claims, not stronger.

challenges.targetPosition: Position 3 (regularity invariants + ratchet, no pre-registered number)
challenges.challenge: 'The forcing function is already satisfied by the null-hypothesis-CUT procedure' is contradicted by the corpus it cites: round2-synthesis-minimal-core.md's Open tensions record that UNDER that very procedure the periphery accreted 'into a second, evidence-free language' and six verdicts each mandated their own drift mechanism. The existing discipline demonstrably failed to bound uncounted growth in one round. The position also commits the offense it polices: the '~2x token variance from prose style' figure is invented with no measurement, and D001 is cited to support a 'measure-then-threshold decision procedure' claim that belongs to the round-2 generics VERDICT, not to the card (which is about Rust monomorphization mechanics).

challenges.targetPosition: Position 3 (regularity invariants + ratchet, no pre-registered number)
challenges.challenge: Point 5's dilemma — a cap is 'either a dead rule (never binds) or wrongly-binding (pressures soundness machinery)' — is a false dilemma against kernel-reserve designs that price floors first and give the gate authority only over merely-beneficial constructs; a gate on the non-floor remainder is neither. And the position's rule-individuation problem is not a side concession but its central weakness: the three binary invariants (one spelling, no context-dependence, empty exception lists) are indeed style-invariant, but the quantitative invariant that does all the budget work ('few', the ratchet) depends on an undefined rule-counting procedure the position admits is gameable via mega-rules and parameterized schemas — so its core superiority claim over token counting (style-invariance) does not hold for the part of its regime that actually replaces the cap.

missingConsiderations: Writer-emitted artifact schemas are uncounted writable surface in all three positions: the AI writer must learn in-context to EMIT provenance records (round2-synthesis line 76, itself flagged EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED) and to consume edit lists, elaborated re-read views, and machine-readable diagnostics. P1's writable-subset definition (grammar + rules + annotation vocabulary + examples) and P2's kernel both exclude these schemas, yet they are part of the description the model needs to operate the language — a shadow spec none of the accounting regimes counts.

Sequencing against the two PENDING OWNER RULINGS (decision-gates.md: D1 quantifier — unconditional vs conditional-on-confined-trusted-base; D0 boundary — human-authored toolchain primitives). These arbitrate the hatch form and the gated-ledger surface, which materially changes the kernel reserve's size. No position sequences budget calibration or kernel-partition drafting after these rulings, though the floor-pricing audit all three implicitly depend on cannot be final before them.

Spec churn economics: the two needs_evidence experiments (interior-annotation regime, interfaces/bounded-quantification), the undesigned FFI wall, and the undesigned concurrency surface will ADD spec mass after any budget/ratchet is adopted. P1 lists late-arriving surface as a risk but no position proposes explicit reserves, versioned re-teaching costs, or a rule for how a hardened number/ratchet absorbs experiment outcomes without the credibility-destroying recalibration all sides fear.

Measurement canonicalization is undefined in all three regimes: token counts are tokenizer- and model-dependent (the round2-syntax regex-proxy lesson), and rule counts lack an individuation procedure (P3 concedes). No position nominates the canonical counting artifact and procedure as an owner decision — without it, every gate's arithmetic is contestable and Goodhartable regardless of which metric wins.

Examples may need a different budget currency than rules: rules interact combinatorially (P1's own interaction-complexity concession) while worked examples are near-additive and D2 explicitly includes them in the description. All three positions price rules and examples in one pool and order the rules-vs-examples ratio experiment, but none draws the accounting consequence — that a single token budget over heterogeneous pedagogical material may be a category error independent of which position wins.

No position gives the UNCOUNTED ledger cut authority: P1 pumps mass toward the toolchain (admitted risk), P2 toward layer specs (admitted 'budget laundering'), P3 toward checked-spec tooling and gate procedures — while the corpus's only documented accretion failure (evidence-free periphery, six duplicate drift mechanisms) occurred precisely on the uncounted side. Registries and policing rules are proposed, but no position specifies a gate with authority to CUT uncounted-side complexity, making all three regimes one-sided by construction.

Cross-model and model-generation drift of the budget's justification: if the deployment window or in-context learning behavior shifts per model generation (P3 raises transfer validity, P1 raises retrieval substitution), any hardened number or measured curve is a snapshot. No position defines re-validation cadence or what happens to already-adjudicated cut decisions when the calibrating measurement goes stale.
result.sharpestTension: Whether the D2 budget binds the language's TOTAL DESCRIPTION or the PER-TASK RESIDENT CONTEXT — and every position's architecture silently picks one before the experiment that distinguishes them has run. Corrected D2's text says the description must be small (few rules, uniform, stated once); but the operational rationale all three positions actually argue from — the spec is taught in-context alongside re-read artifacts, edit lists, and the program under repair — is a resident-share argument. Position 1's whole-spec cap is only coherent if total description is the binding variable; Position 2's kernel+layers architecture is only coherent if resident share is (it explicitly GROWS total description — extension law, registry, provisioning protocol — to shrink residency); Position 3 refuses to enshrine either because the arbitrating experiments (spec-size ablation, retrieval-substitution, context-competition: P1's experiments 1/3/5, P2's U1/U7, P3's experiments 2/5) are unrun and the corpus has zero AI-codegen cards. These readings force incompatible architectures — monolith-with-cap versus kernel-plus-retrieved-layers — and D2's text alone cannot arbitrate, because its stated rationale (in-context teaching) is precisely the part that is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED. Compounding it: all three regimes enforce compactness only over a counted subset while relocating unpriced mass to an uncounted ledger, which is the exact accretion channel round-2's synthesis names as its live failure mode. The debate is therefore not budget-versus-no-budget; it is which uncarded empirical premise about model learning gets engraved as architecture before anyone has measured it — and none of the three positions can win without pre-empting the experiment program all three claim to defer to.

positionAssessments.position: Familiarity-first surface within the fixed regularity floors (Rust-adjacent lexemes AND Rust-shaped node/grammar forms, guarded by a prior-alignment invariant; every semantic delta spelled visibly novel)
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly identifies that the round-2 floors already fix rule-set regularity, so the remaining choice is which single spelling each construct gets; the prior-alignment invariant ('familiar token only where checked semantics match the prior') is a principled contamination guard; the observation that the core's semantics are Rust-shaped by decree (D1 reference bar, F001 exclusivity, C004 capabilities, D001 mono-generics, Result+trap, zero implicit conversions) is accurate and card-backed; concessions are honest and it pre-commits a kill condition (fast penalty decay kills the position). Its error-class analysis (transcription-distance vs precedence-misbinding) engages the record's own named hazards rather than inventing new ones.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Post-critique its distinctive content shrinks to the D2-priced part: the spec-neutral half (token strings) collapses into Position 3, while the Rust-shaped node inventory (braces/arrows/statement-like forms, more productions) is exactly what corrected D2 charges — contradicting its own 'approximately spec-mass-neutral' headline (its concession 1 admits this). 'Weakly dominates on both silent-error classes' is empirical dressed as structural: mandatory full parenthesization and per-node mode qualifiers are themselves re-encoding steps, so class (a) is reduced, not eliminated. Its central claim is NOT the carded experiment's measurand — the filed Syntax A/B is two-arm (verified: backlog line 43, canonical vs infix); the familiar-lexeme-canonical arm exists in no backlog item and its 'pre-committed kill thresholds' exist nowhere. The prior-alignment invariant is one sentence of spec but N fuzzy match judgments against Rust-as-trained (informal, edition-drifting, Nomicon-partial per caveats.md) with an admittedly unowned delta audit — a governance-level special-case generator. Shape-level prior predictions are wrong almost everywhere under uniform mandatory annotation (no ?, no closures, no elision), so the transfer channel is narrower than the rhetoric. Cites D010 without the required unverified flag (verified: round3-synthesis no-votes list).

positionAssessments.position: Regularity-first: maximally uniform canonical surface even if alien; familiar tokens admissible only as isolated rule-free lexemes; familiarity purchasable later only as the gated bijective write-codec
positionAssessments.strengths: Under a rule-count reading of D2 its argmin claim is analytic and the grammar-mass comparison is computable today without a model in the loop; fewer productions genuinely shrink the frontend trust path at the F003 soundness chokepoint (N006 prices each rewrite at proof grade; F006/SPARK is real restrict-expressiveness precedent); its steelman point 5 (adopt familiar rule-free lexemes) removes the maximal-alienness strawman; and its no-regret ordering argument is the strongest structural argument that survived critique — the parked bijective codec keeps familiarity purchasable later as tooling, while baking familiar forms into the stored canonical artifact is a language break to undo after artifacts and teaching examples accumulate.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its decisive third argument — 'regular-alien fails LOUD' — is contradicted by the record it cites: round2-syntax names a silent, typechecks-and-verifier-invisible class specific to the alien form (transcription-distance operand-order/associativity swaps), so both surfaces own a record-named silent class and the detectability asymmetry is not architectural. Its flagship exhibit (Rust-looking bare '+' lowering to a different contract) is unrepresentable under floors every position accepts (one named mode per arithmetic node); its own concession 3 admits the mandatory-explicit-facts floor is the load-bearing defense and alienness only second-order. It prices a 13+-level precedence table, sugar, elision, and multiple byte forms against an opponent who forswears all of them — a substantial strawman burden. 'Half this debate is decidable by arithmetic' overreaches because user-directives line 16 places examples INSIDE the description that must be small, so production-counting settles rule mass only. Deploys D009 as load-bearing without the required unverified flag (verified: round3-synthesis no-votes list). After its own steelman, its live disagreement with Position 3 is nearly nil.

positionAssessments.position: Lexicon/grammar split: keep the round-2 alien-regular canonical grammar untouched; borrow the lexicon (keywords, type names, annotation vocabulary) from the Rust/C prior where semantics substantially match, mint fresh names where they diverge, de-alias every borrowed name
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly dissolves the debate's false dichotomy along the load-bearing seam: regularity lives in the grammar (already decided law — round2-syntax; A001's negative scope leaves lexicon genuinely undecided), familiarity lives in the token strings, and lexicon choice is spec-budget-neutral to first order. Its strongest post-critique survival is a genuine D2-INTERNAL argument no other position has: the spec's own teaching examples are written in the surface language and sit inside the protected in-context budget, so lexicon token-efficiency touches the D2 metric directly, not just behavior. The observation that the design record already conducts all discussion in Rust's metalanguage (Send/Sync, Result, monomorphization) is true and verifiable in every round file. It hedges both record-named silent-error classes without needing to know which dominates, and its relocation defense (per-node mode tokens deny the wrap-assumption a silent landing site) is sound under decided floors.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critique lands three real hits: (1) internal contradiction — if spec definitions are fully self-contained, the 'differs from Rust' delta lines are redundant mass; if they are load-bearing, the spec is conditioned on an unverified drifting prior (definition-by-delta, which its own concession disclaims); it must pick self-contained-plus-mnemonics and forfeit the delta lines' claimed function. (2) Its showcase contains an actual false friend: Rust's unchecked_* arithmetic is UB-on-overflow while the corresponding promise here is nsw/nuw-POISON, a distinction N001's ladder makes load-bearing — the 'pre-stabilized vocabulary' can import a semantic blur at exactly a divergence point, and the round2-type-inference line 30 'pre-stabilized' gloss confuses fixed strings with stable name-to-semantics mappings. (3) The BPE single-token claim is unchecked (its own E3) and stacked against a strawman — an alien lexicon can also use short single-token ASCII atoms. 'For most of the vocabulary the Rust name is the TRUE name' rests on a divergence census that has never been run, and the conceded residual silent channel (wrong-but-legal token selection under prior habit) is precisely the class the strict grammar cannot catch.

keyTradeoffs: D2 unit of measure (the sharpest tension, correctly identified by the critic): under a rule-count/irregularity reading (user-directives line 18) Position 2 wins by definition before any experiment; under a total in-context teaching-footprint reading (line 16 places 'examples' inside the description that must be small) Positions 1/3 can win — but only via 100% EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED example-efficiency claims. Both readings are textually supported; no one has asked the owner to fix the unit, and the positions are incommensurable until it is fixed.

Node-shape inventory (the only grammar question actually live): Rust-shaped forms buy an untested prior-transfer benefit — bounded above by the skeleton's share of tokens in a round-3-envelope elaborated artifact, which nobody has measured — at a spec-mass cost that is computable today by drafting both grammars and counting productions.

Lexicon borrowing: buys real BPE compactness of the spec's own in-context examples plus true-name alignment for semantics that are Rust's by decree, at the price of a de-alias table proportional to an unrun divergence census plus the one silent channel the canonical grammar cannot convert to a hard error (wrong-but-legal token selection at divergence points, e.g. unchecked-UB vs poison).

Silent-error symmetry: each surface owns a record-named verifier-invisible typechecks-but-wrong class — alien: transcription-distance operand-order/associativity swaps (round2-syntax); familiar: prior-import token selection — while the decided floors (per-node modes, canonical single spelling, grammar-rejected unlicensed forms) partially defuse BOTH flagship exhibits. The decisive quantity is the relative rate of two unmeasured silent classes, and the measurement has a shelf life across model generations shorter than the irreversibility of the decision it settles.

Reversibility asymmetry: an alien-regular canonical core keeps familiarity purchasable later as the parked gated bijective codec (tooling, zero spec cost); baking familiar grammar shapes into the stored canonical form is a language break to undo after artifacts and teaching examples accumulate. Lexicon choice, however, is sticky in BOTH directions (renaming is a breaking gated vocabulary event), so the codec escape hatch de-risks the grammar-shape decision far more than the lexicon decision.

Token-frequency benefit and autocomplete-contamination hazard are the same coin: the tokenizer statistics that make 'fn'/'match' cheap single tokens are what make Rust continuations mechanically high-probability after them — no brief weighed these jointly; the experiments must measure them on the same arm.

Falsifiability asymmetry (critic's most consequential addition): with a familiar lexicon, a passing teachability eval cannot distinguish spec-driven from prior-driven competence — 'the spec+examples suffice' is testable only on the alien rendering. Spec bugs masked by prior compensation surface only when the prior shifts; this is a long-term assurance cost of familiarity no position priced.
evidenceGaps: Zero AI-codegen evidence cards (round-2 standing flag; caveats.md standing rule): every prior-transfer, penalty-decay, false-friend, contamination, and example-budget claim in all three briefs is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

The filed Syntax A/B (missing-research-backlog.jsonl line 43 — verified this session) is two-arm, canonical vs infix; the familiar-lexeme-canonical arm that Position 1's and Position 3's cases ride on exists in no backlog item, and no pre-committed kill thresholds for it exist anywhere.

Four computable-now, model-free artifacts have never been produced: (a) the spec-mass arithmetic (draft both grammar specs, count productions and exception clauses); (b) the real-BPE tokenizer census E3 (the 1.35-1.48x figure is a flagged regex proxy on fragments); (c) the divergence census (which Rust names are true names vs false friends — prerequisite for placing the false-friend battery's traps); (d) the skeleton-share census (fraction of tokens in a representative round-3-envelope elaborated artifact covered by any Rust prior — upper-bounds the familiarity benefit).

D009 and D010 remain direct-extraction with no adversarial-verification votes (verified: round3-synthesis no-votes list; the 2026-07-02 log covers only D001 and C004), yet both were deployed load-bearing without flags — one citation-discipline violation per side.

Neither silent error class has a measured rate: alien transcription-distance swaps and familiar prior-import token selection are both hypothesized only; no differential-testing data, no mixed-dialect (uncanny valley) data, no penalty-decay curves per model generation.

The owner has not ruled on the D2 unit of measure (rule count only vs total spec+examples in-context footprint) — a directive-interpretation gap, not an empirical one, and the single item that determines which position's arithmetic is even admissible.

Repair-loop exposure effects are unmodeled: every diagnostic/edit-list iteration feeds the model more canonical-form text, so production penalty decay may be dominated by session-accumulated exposure rather than curated examples — this confounds both the persistent-tax risk and the pre-committed kill conditions unless the experiment controls for it.

The marginal value of surface familiarity CONDITIONAL on the decided tooling architecture (node-path machine diagnostics, site-enumerated edit lists, elaborated re-read views) is a different quantity from raw generation familiarity and is the one that matters here; no experiment as specified isolates it.

The elaborator-as-codec / source-of-record tension (round2-synthesis open tension; round2-type-inference) is unresolved: if interior elision wins its experiment, the write surface and re-read surface diverge, and familiarity/regularity could be split across them — all three positions assumed one surface serves both directions.
result.leadingCandidate: Amended Position 3 (lexicon/grammar split): the round-2 alien-regular canonical grammar stands untouched (uniform node shape — Position 1's Rust-shaped node inventory stays out unless the three-arm experiment shows a large, persistent penalty for the uniform shape); the lexicon is selectively borrowed from the Rust prior ONLY where a completed divergence census shows substantial semantic match, with fully self-contained spec definitions (familiar names as mnemonics only — no definition-by-delta, no load-bearing 'differs from Rust' lines), minted novel tokens at every divergence point (numeric-mode vocabulary explicitly minted, not borrowed: Rust's unchecked_* is UB where ours is poison), and one written borrow-vs-mint policy stated once with a named owner.
result.rationale: The critique collapses the debate to something much narrower than the question as posed. Grammar regularity is not actually at stake: round-2 canonical form (one spelling, no sugar, no precedence, no elision, byte-canonical, tree-isomorphic) is decided law, Position 1 operates inside it, and Position 2's own steelman admits rule-free familiar lexemes — so the live variables are only (1) node-shape inventory and (2) lexicon policy. On (1), Position 1's distinctive claim survives only as an untested behavioral bet: its spec-neutrality headline contradicts its own concession, its dominance argument is empirical not structural (parenthesization and mode qualifiers are still re-encoding steps), and its central experiment arm is unfiled — while Position 2's reversibility argument survives critique intact: the parked gated codec makes familiarity purchasable later, whereas Rust-shaped canonical grammar is a language break to undo. On (2), Position 3 correctly prices the terrain (lexicon is first-order spec-neutral; the spec's own examples sit inside the protected D2 budget; much of the vocabulary names semantics that are Rust's by decree), but its showcase contains a confirmed false friend and its census is unrun, so borrowing must be census-gated and self-contained. Position 2's loud/silent asymmetry — its claimed escape from the empirical deadlock — fails on the record's own text: both surfaces own a named verifier-invisible silent class, and the decided floors partially defuse both flagship exhibits. What actually decides the residue is the relative rate of two unmeasured silent error classes plus the penalty-decay slope and example-budget differential — pure model behavior with zero cards — hence research_needed. The amended split is the leading candidate because it is the only position robust to the experiments' outcome: if familiarity proves worthless, the cost was a small de-alias table inside an unchanged grammar; if it proves valuable, nearly all of the harvestable benefit (lexicon plus spec-example compactness) is already captured without the irreversible grammar-shape bet. Two non-empirical items must precede any hardening: the owner ruling fixing D2's unit of measure, and the four computable-now censuses that make the model experiments interpretable.
followUps: Request an owner ruling fixing the D2 unit of measure and record it in notes/user-directives.md: does the 'description that must be small' count ONLY rule count/irregularity (line 18 reading) or the TOTAL spec+examples in-context footprint (line 16 reading, examples included)? This is decision-critical: rule-count-only decides for regularity-first by arithmetic; total-footprint makes the experiments decisive.

Run the four computable-now, model-free artifacts BEFORE any model experiment, each with a named owner: (a) spec-mass arithmetic — draft both grammar specs (uniform-wrapper vs Rust-shaped node inventory, both zero-sugar/zero-precedence/byte-canonical) and count productions plus exception clauses; (b) tokenizer census (P3's E3) — real BPE counts across current major tokenizers on matched spec+examples+programs under three lexicons: Rust-familiar, short-single-token-ASCII-alien (not strawman fragmenting atoms), and mixed; (c) divergence census — enumerate every candidate borrowed name, classify substantial-match vs divergence against dated Rust-stable documentation, size the de-alias table, and card the unchecked-UB-vs-poison false friend explicitly against N001; (d) skeleton-share census — fraction of tokens in a representative round-3-envelope fully-elaborated artifact covered by any Rust prior, which upper-bounds the total familiarity benefit.

File the three-arm surface experiment as a NEW backlog item (the existing Syntax A/B at missing-research-backlog.jsonl line 43 is two-arm and gates only the infix codec): arms (i) alien-uniform canonical, (ii) familiar-lexicon canonical (amended P3), (iii) Rust-shaped-node canonical (P1); identical-length in-context specs; real BPE tokenizers; whole programs. Metrics: first-parse success, examples-to-accuracy-threshold, penalty-decay slope per arm, generation tokens, repair iterations to green. Pre-commit kill thresholds IN THE FILING, e.g.: if arm (i)'s defect rate converges to within an agreed epsilon of arm (ii)'s within <=5 in-context examples, familiarity is a transition subsidy and the uniform surface wins; if arm (iii) shows no persistent advantage over (ii), the Rust-shaped node inventory is permanently rejected.

Run the silent-error differential battery (requires the divergence census first for trap placement): seed divergence-point tasks (mode tokens, no '?', no wildcard arms, explicit instantiation, no implicit conversions) and measure per arm (a) grammar-caught unlicensed-form emission, (b) wrong-but-legal token selection (silent), and (c) transcription-distance operand-order/associativity swap rate via differential testing against reference implementations — this measures the two record-named silent classes head-to-head, the actual decisive quantity of the debate.

Add a repair-loop exposure-decay probe to the experiment protocol: multi-turn regeneration with machine diagnostics; measure Rust-pattern fix-import rates per lexicon arm AND whether session-accumulated canonical-form text dominates penalty decay over curated spec examples — without this control the pre-committed kill thresholds in the three-arm filing are confounded.

Record the falsifiability asymmetry as a standing caveat in notes/caveats.md and as a design consequence: because familiar-arm teachability evals cannot distinguish spec-driven from prior-driven competence, the spec-completeness validation suite MUST include the alien-lexicon rendering even if a familiar lexicon ships — the alien rendering is the only channel that tests whether the spec is actually self-contained.

Complete the adversarial-verification sweep on D009 and D010 (both on the round3-synthesis no-votes list, both deployed load-bearing and unflagged in this debate) before the surface decision hardens, and propagate the unverified-flag discipline of round2-type-inference line 31 into future surface briefs.

Assign ownership of the standing governance artifacts this topic creates: the written borrow-vs-mint policy (one policy, stated once, applied uniformly), the divergence census maintenance, and the delta/prior-alignment audit that Position 1 admitted is unowned — an unowned audit converts every census miss into the silent-capture failure mode.

Resolve the write-surface/read-surface question jointly with the interior-elision experiment and the parked codec policy (round2-synthesis open tension: elaborator-as-codec): decide which artifact is source of record during repair loops BEFORE interpreting any surface experiment, since familiarity and regularity could otherwise be split across the surface the model writes and the canonical form it re-reads.

positionAssessments.position: P1 — Hard CI-enforced whole-spec budget (10-20k token anchor, kernel reserve priced first, calibration audit before cut authority)
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly identifies the governance gap: D2 names spec description-length/regularity the first-class metric yet nothing enforces it, and the corpus's own recorded failure (round2-synthesis Open tensions: periphery accreting into 'a second, evidence-free language', six uncoordinated drift mechanisms) shows the existing null-hypothesis-CUT discipline did not bound uncounted growth. Its procedural apparatus survives the critique intact: counted token+rule deltas on every design change, whole-spec DIFFERENTIAL accounting (a construct that deletes N ad-hoc rules is correctly kept), kernel-reserve-priced-first sequencing, the no-shadow-spec rule, and spec-as-CI-artifact — which is also the missing prerequisite for the syntax A/B, drift, and every in-context experiment round 2 already ordered. The dual-support argument partially survives: a smaller writable surface genuinely shrinks the F003 frontend soundness chokepoint and the N006-priced validation program even if token count is the wrong unit. Its concessions are honest and mostly correct.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The headline number does not survive its own concessions: the 10-20k anchor is uncarded (no one has tokenized Lua 5.4, R7RS-small, or the drafted floors with a real BPE tokenizer), the position admits full manuals plausibly bust 20k, and its own risk analysis (a moved number permanently damages credibility) argues against pre-registering a number it expects to move. The retrodiction claim ('the budget reproduces the round-2 verdict lattice') was never computed and is one-sided — round 2 also MANDATED spec-heavy writer-visible machinery (typed operation tables with coherence rules, contract-group kernel, FFI declaration frames, named mode vocabulary, env-struct diagnostics). Card overreach confirmed by the critic: C004's confirmed scope is data-race prevention only (07-02 amendment sharpened it), not evidence the full D1 kernel (ownership/borrow system, obligation ledger, FFI frames) fits any budget; N006 prices optimizer folds, not spec tokens — the carded benefit maps to rule/production count, collapsing most of the carded case into P3's regime. Internal contradiction between 'spec-cost as the tie-breaker NOW' for the open tensions and concession 5's 'pre-registered input to experiments only' is unresolved. What survives is 'hard-in-procedure, not hard-in-threshold' — operationally near-identical to P3 plus a premature constant.

positionAssessments.position: P2 — Kernel-scoped hard budget + on-demand layered specs (conservative-extension law, census-gated 80% kernel path)
positionAssessments.strengths: Names the real deployment pressure the others under-weight: the spec shares context with the mandatory re-read artifact, site-enumerated edit lists, machine-readable diagnostics, and the program under repair (round2-synthesis 'Additions' section), so resident share is bounded whether designed or discovered. Several components have standalone value regardless of layering: the conservative-extension law (layers only add vocabulary at kernel extension points; unknown constructs are deterministic hard errors under canonical-form + closed-world, so under-provisioning costs iterations, never soundness), spec provisioning recorded in provenance, content-addressed version-pinned spec artifacts, and the observation that round-3's overflow ruling (wrap fallback legal) supplies a kernel-safe default arithmetic. Its U1-U8 empirical program is the best-specified of the three and should largely be adopted as the experiment battery.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The architecture answers resident-context share while D2's text constrains TOTAL description — layering GROWS total normative mass (extension law, layer registry, provisioning protocol, per-layer budgets, version-pinning) to shrink residency, a trade rational only if its own untested premises U1/U7 hold; it concedes it abandons a hard bound on total spec size. The kernel/layer partition collides with decided floors at named points: storage/lifetime contracts are mandatory on EVERY allocation (round2-synthesis line 29) — majority-path surface, not layer material — and typed operation tables are the blessed CORE dispatch form (line 27). Citation defects verified against the corpus: 'F001 CONFIRMED 2026-07-02' is false (the 07-02 set is A001/A002/A006/C004/D001/D002/D003/D005/D007/J004); D004 says nothing about spec serving; D009 is provisional and documents performance degradation, not silent semantic reinterpretation; the N001 flagship example was never writable-subset mass (the UB/poison ladder is compiler-internal under either architecture). The 80%-kernel claim rests on a census (U4) that does not exist, and layer-membership knowledge is itself uncounted description mass of exactly the context-dependent kind D2 taxes. Premature as adopted architecture; valuable as an experiment arm.

positionAssessments.position: P3 — No pre-registered number: machine-checkable regularity invariants (one spelling, zero context-dependence, empty exception lists, stated-once) + ratcheted rule count; numeric budget only measurement-derived
positionAssessments.strengths: Most faithful operationalization of corrected D2's literal text — the invariants map one-to-one onto the directive's enumerated budget-eaters (sugar, alternate spellings, inference, elision, special cases) and are style- and tokenizer-invariant, avoiding re-enshrining the exact proxy failure the corpus already recorded (round2-syntax's 1.35-1.48x regex-proxy lesson). Matches the project's demonstrated decision procedure (measure-then-threshold with pre-committed gates, per the round-2 verdicts) and caveats.md's standing rule: with zero in-context-acquisition cards, any specific number silently engraves an untested model-behavior claim as law. Its Goodhart analysis of a binding cap is credible and specific: the cheapest tokens to cut are exactly what D2 values (worked examples, fully-spelled quantifiers), and compression pressure converts explicit statements into denser context-dependent formulations — irregularity increased while the metric improves. The spec-as-checked-artifact and spec-regression-ratchet mechanics are adoptable immediately and force composition-over-addition, which is D2's uniformity demand made structural.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The ratchet inherits the arbitrariness it condemns one level down: initial per-layer rule bounds are judgment numbers with zero acquisition data, rule individuation is undefined and gameable (mega-rules, parameterized schemas, giant uniform tables), and a ratchet binds the delta from an unmeasured level — it cannot detect that the level is already too high. Governance asymmetry cuts against it: caps fail loudly, ratchet gates fail quietly, and its learning-curve harness is a critical-path dependency whose delay forces stalled evolution or a waived gate (its own words: worse than a crude cap). Its 'forcing function already satisfied' claim is contradicted by the corpus it cites — periphery accretion happened UNDER the existing discipline. It commits the offense it polices: the ~2x prose-variance figure is invented, and D001 is cited for a procedural claim that belongs to the generics verdict, not the card. Its style-invariance superiority claim holds for the three binary invariants but not for the quantitative part ('few', the ratchet) that actually replaces the cap.

keyTradeoffs: Pre-registered number vs measured-later number: a hard constant is cheap to adjudicate, fails loudly, and forces real prioritization fights now — but with zero acquisition cards it engraves an untested model-behavior claim, invites Goodhart compression (example deletion, rule-merging into context-dependence), and a later recalibration permanently damages the gate's 'hard' credibility; a measured-later number is evidence-honest but fails quietly and stalls if the learning-curve harness slips.

Total-description vs resident-context accounting — the sharpest tension: a monolith-with-cap is coherent only if total description binds; kernel-plus-retrieved-layers is coherent only if resident share binds (it grows total description to shrink residency). D2's text says total; its stated rationale (in-context teaching) is a residency argument; the arbitrating experiments (spec-size ablation, context-competition, retrieval-substitution) are unrun. Adopting either architecture now pre-empts the experiment all sides claim to defer to.

Token count vs rule/production count as the gated unit: tokens capture everything D2 names (including examples) but are tokenizer-dependent and prose-gameable; rule counts are style-invariant and map to the carded costs (F003 frontend soundness surface, N006 proof-grade validation per rule) but lack an individuation procedure and are mega-rule-gameable. Neither suffices alone; both need a canonical counting procedure that no position defines.

Counted-ledger discipline vs uncounted-ledger accretion: every regime relocates mass to its uncounted side (P1 to toolchain, P2 to layer specs, P3 to gate procedures and checked-spec tooling), yet the corpus's only documented accretion failure occurred precisely on the uncounted side; registries are proposed but no position grants cut authority over uncounted complexity, making all three one-sided by construction.

Rules vs examples in one budget pool: rules interact combinatorially (P1's own interaction-complexity concession) while worked examples are near-additive and explicitly part of D2's teaching channel; a single budget over heterogeneous pedagogical material risks optimizing away the cheaper-per-token teaching medium — the exchange rate is untested (examples-ratio ablation).

Budget as tie-breaker for the open tensions vs experiment deferral: spec-cost is the only measurable criterion available NOW for interfaces/closures/sharing (P1's strongest practical pitch), but round 2 ruled those 'empirically unarbitrable today' with ordered experiments and pre-committed thresholds; a budget-broken tie that pre-empts a needs_evidence verdict must carry a recorded reopening condition or it silently overrides decided procedure.
evidenceGaps: Zero AI-codegen / in-context-acquisition evidence cards exist (round2-syntax records this explicitly and orders a new lane that is still unopened). Every load-bearing learnability claim — spec-size degradation knee, irregularity-vs-size dominance, context competition, retrieval substitution, rules-vs-examples exchange rate, cross-model transfer — is EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED.

No calibration measurements exist: Lua 5.4, R7RS-small, and (critically) a drafted round-2-floors + round-3-envelope spec have never been priced in real BPE tokens or counted rules. P1's 10-20k anchor, P1's retrodiction claim, and P2's 80%-kernel partition are all unpriced assertions; the floor-pricing audit is the cheapest missing artifact and blocks all three positions.

Kernel-reserve size is unknown and moving: the FFI wall (research_needed, zero primary-source cards), concurrency surface (C004 kernel undesigned), region/arena contracts, and the two needs_evidence outcomes (interior annotations, interfaces/bounded quantification) will add spec mass after any regime hardens. Note the D1a ruling's checker-simplification levers (explicit regions/borrows over inference, lexical borrows, minimal Featherweight-Rust/Oxide-class calculus) plausibly SHRINK the ownership spec description — also unpriced.

No canonical counting procedure is defined anywhere: which tokenizer(s), how rules are individuated (schema vs instantiations, table rows, quantified rule families), and what counts as normative. Without it every gate's arithmetic is contestable regardless of which metric wins — this is a procedural/owner decision, not an experiment.

Writer-emitted artifact schemas are uncounted shadow spec in all three regimes: the writer must learn in-context to emit provenance records (itself flagged EMPIRICAL-UNTESTED at round2-synthesis line 76) and to consume edit lists, elaborated re-read views, and machine-readable diagnostics — description mass excluded from P1's writable subset and P2's kernel alike.

challenges.challenge: Your allocation argument misreads corrected D2's object. D2 (user-directives.md line 16) prices the language DESCRIPTION itself — 'few rules, uniform, no special cases, each stated once' — not the per-task context payload. Your position presupposes a 'long tail of rule elaborations, interaction cases, and edge semantics' warehoused in a citation database; if that long tail exists, the language already violates D2 regardless of where the text is stored, and lazy delivery discharges nothing. Conversely, if D2 holds and the rule set is genuinely small, the eager-vs-lazy delta shrinks toward noise and the allocation argument loses its magnitude. Worse, repair-loop-primary removes the cost pressure that enforces D2: rules become cheap to add because 'the checker will teach them' — an accretion vector your spec-distillation gate polices in the wrong direction (it gates distillation INTO the kernel, not rule-count growth in the language).

The rule-to-diagnostic bijection is broken by decided architecture you never cite: D1a's adopted checker-simplification levers (decision-gates.md line 74) include 'soundness-over-completeness — checker may reject sound-but-hard patterns and demand restructuring' plus an Austral/Featherweight-class minimal calculus. That guarantees a structural lesson class where a rejection cites NO violated spec rule and has NO mechanical fix — the checker is saying 'this is sound but I cannot see it; restructure.' Your core equation (diagnostic = spec citation + witness + edit list) cannot represent this class; your concession 4 admits restructure-hint diagnostics are undesigned; and for exactly the nonlocal ownership errors everyone concedes are hardest, this class is plausibly the dominant iteration driver, not a tail case. The teacher fails precisely where teaching is most needed.

Your F003 citation inverts the card. F003's content (and its use everywhere in the record — round2-syntax line 15, Position 2's own citation) marks the frontend fact-emission boundary as the pipeline's soundness HAZARD: facts are sound only if frontend obligations are true. It is not evidence that checker verdicts are 'trustworthy enough to serve as the teaching source of record.' Combined with your own risk 1 (a wrong citation actively teaches a false rule), the position converts the entire diagnostic-text database into TCB-adjacent surface demanding N006-grade tested-and-versioned discipline — N006 carries design_generalization_confidence: low, and this maintenance cost is unpriced and carried only by your position.

The dynamic half of your teacher rides an unclosed spec. Layer-1 bounds checks — the highest-volume D1-critical class under proven-else-checked — teach only at runtime via trap=abort (one lesson per process death, conceded), but round-3 residuals record that trap semantics under racing threads and relaxed-trap-ordering are UNSPECIFIED (round3-synthesis lines 53-54), so runtime lesson attribution and atomicity are undefined. Also, 'the same defect always yields the same diagnostic bytes, so repair converges' states a proposed obligation plus an untested model-behavior claim (your own risk 3 names oscillation/livelock) as an established property.

Your leg-2 record claim is manufactured. Round 2 decided a diagnostics FLOOR (node-path references, edit lists, aggregation, single-hop naming — lines 55-56, 66, 69), nowhere a ceiling: no round text says diagnostics were 'deliberately stripped of explanatory content' or forbids rule-text payloads. Your own obligation (b) — rule-cited diagnostics — proves you believe the channel is extensible, which is incoherent with claiming that extending it 'contradicts the diagnostic design the rounds already decided.' The record is silent; you are litigating silence as decided law.

Your amortization economics inverts D2. 'One spec line amortizes to zero marginal cost' treats in-context spec text as free, but D2 exists precisely because the in-context description is the scarce, per-task-paid resource — every agent pays the full spec payload on every task whether or not the rule is relevant, which is the exact cost structure Position 1 attacks. Your concession 5 admits the budget binds; leg 4's fleet arithmetic double-counts round-trip costs against the loop while zero-counting context costs against the spec. The comparison is unmeasured in both directions and your framing presents one direction as settled.

The silent-wrong asymmetry — your strongest leg — does not actually route the burden to the spec. Your own citations show the record's decided remedies for the named silent-wrong classes were BY-CONSTRUCTION design-away (auto-derived Result context, round2-error-handling) and differential testing, not spec prose; transcription-slip classes (operand-order swaps, round2-syntax line 19) are attention failures no spec paragraph prevents; and whether spec text produces first-pass semantic correctness on a novel language at all is your own flagged-untested foundation. The asymmetry argues for a third channel — language design plus a differential/silent-wrong harness — which your position then absorbs as new toolchain obligations (f, g) no round has authorized, while claiming the conclusion 'can only live in the spec.'

The irregularity-meter (leg 3) conflates three causes it cannot distinguish: a persistent hot diagnostic can mean (a) D2-irregularity, (b) prior interference against a perfectly uniform rule (Position 3's anti-prior class — uniformity does not neutralize Rust/C-family priors), or (c) D1a's decided checker incompleteness rejecting sound patterns. 'With high probability exactly what D2 calls irregular' is an uncarded distributional claim about model behavior presented as the operational core of your program; routing all persistent error mass to language-redesign review misdiagnoses (b) and (c), and your threshold-and-concede policy has no discriminator among them.

'The only position whose central mechanism converts that claim into a continuously measured quantity' is false on the face of the rival cases: Position 1 ships loop instrumentation (iterations-to-green, repeat-violation rates) as first-class output and a frequency-driven migration dial; Position 2 ships fleet diagnostic telemetry and a measured spec-ROI curve. Your genuine distinctive is CI-GATING language changes on current-model pass rates — which is exactly the process-relative acceptance that round-3 cross-cutting invariant 4 resists and line 42 names as the architecture's one normative residue. You concede this, then still claim the epistemic high ground that belongs equally to all three positions.

'The teaching pack is D2's regression test' is a category error. D2's metric is IRREGULARITY, not token mass: a uniform verbose-everywhere rule is owner-ruled spec-cheap yet grows the pack, while an irregular context-dependent elision rule can be textually short yet is exactly what D2 bans. Pack-size deltas measure bytes; benchmark pass-rate deltas measure current-model difficulty — gating D2 on either entangles the constraint with 2026-model priors (your own model-idiom lock-in risk) and quietly rewrites an owner directive into a proxy the owner did not state. Similarly, calling anti-prior teaching 'exactly the special-case text D2 bans' conflates language irregularity with pedagogical annotation about model priors; D2 bans the former, and merely prices the latter.

'Complete' is unsupported anywhere. Also 'the curriculum literally cannot drift out of sync with the language' covers only extracted code subtrees; the kernel prose, diagnostics explanations, and anti-prior notes are not re-verified subtrees, and pack-faithfulness validation is your own conceded undesigned deliverable — the normative half of the pack can rot exactly like hand-written docs.

Declaring novel free-text errors 'toolchain bugs' before the vocabulary's domain is specifiable makes the guarantee vacuous or the gate a permanent blocker — and your bootstrap-circularity concession (coverage-forcing dilutes 'examples are real verified code') already concedes the purity claim the position leads with.

result.sharpestTension: The debate's core quantity is undefined and its arbitration metric is misaligned, and the two defects compound. First, corrected D2 prices the language DESCRIPTION (few rules total, uniform, stated once), and the two principal positions exploit the ambiguity in opposite directions: P1 reads D2 as a per-task context-delivery budget so a rule long-tail can be warehoused in the checker's citation database (but if that tail exists the language violates D2 wherever the text lives), while P2 reads the spec as amortizing to zero marginal cost (but in-context spec space is exactly the scarce resource D2 prices). Until the owner rules whether D2 binds the artifact or the payload, 'how much burden moves' has no defined unit and the shared arbitration experiment cannot be scored. Second, whatever wins that experiment will have optimized checker acceptance and iterations-to-green — yet the checker can only teach what it rejects, and the project's binding objective (D0 extreme performance plus the record's named silent-wrong classes) is rejection-invisible by construction; its only proposed teachers, lint channels and differential testing, hang on the single flagged-untested assumption that models act on non-error feedback (round2-synthesis line 104), and D1a's decided soundness-over-completeness checker adds a growing lesson class (sound-but-rejected restructure demands) that is neither a spec rule nor a mechanical fix. So both allocation positions concede, from opposite sides, that the dimension that actually matters has no teacher, no owner, and no authorized harness — the debate is fighting over who delivers the rules while the objective sits outside the delivery channel entirely.

positionAssessments.position: Repair-loop-primary (kernel spec + checker-as-teacher via citation-grade diagnostics)
positionAssessments.strengths: Its toolchain-guarantee set largely survives independent of its allocation claim: stable content-addressed rule IDs, diagnostics carrying canonical rule text + node-path witness + edit list, determinism/byte-stability, one-defect-one-repair-site, rule-to-emitter coverage audit, and loop instrumentation are all cheap extensions of machinery round 2 already mandates for correctness (round2-synthesis lines 66-76), and the decided floors (canonical-form hard errors, zero implicit conversion/inference, banned wildcards, no writer-emittable trusted assertions) genuinely make acceptance track spec-conformance for checker-visible rules — the precondition ordinary languages lack (F006 apt; D009 apt as contrast, though unverified). Honest concessions; only position that designed the migration dial in detail.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Three critic hits land hard. (1) D2 binds the language DESCRIPTION as an artifact (user-directives line 16 verified): if a 'long tail of rule elaborations, interaction cases, edge semantics' exists to warehouse in a citation database, the language already violates D2 wherever the text lives; under a D2-compliant few-rules language the eager-vs-lazy delta shrinks and the allocation argument loses magnitude — worse, lazy delivery removes the cost pressure that enforces the rule cap. (2) The rule-to-diagnostic bijection is broken by decided architecture it never cites: the adopted soundness-over-completeness lever (decision-gates line 74, verified) guarantees rejections that cite no violated rule and admit no mechanical fix (restructure demands), concentrated exactly on the nonlocal ownership errors everyone concedes are hardest; its diagnostic taxonomy cannot represent this class. (3) F003 is cited inverted (the card marks the frontend fact boundary as the soundness HAZARD, not a trust warrant), and 'same defect → same bytes → repair converges' states an untested model-behavior claim as fact while its own risk 3 concedes livelock is undesigned-against. The dynamic half rides unspecified concurrent-trap semantics (round3 lines 53-54), and diagnostic text becomes an unpriced N006-grade teaching TCB.

positionAssessments.position: Spec-primary (compact uniform spec carries the burden; loop = safety net, mechanical executor, telemetry)
positionAssessments.strengths: Owns the argument that survives even if loops become free: the silent-wrong asymmetry. The checker can only teach what it rejects, and the record's own verdicts enumerate checker-invisible classes — region pessimization/leak holes (round2-synthesis lines 60, 74), context-dropping Result variants (round2-error-handling), operand-order swaps (round2-syntax), and performance-wrong code generally, which is D0's actual objective — so a nonzero irreducible eager burden exists regardless of loop economics. Extending round-3 invariant 4 (artifact-checkable, not process-relative) to pedagogy is a genuine architectural harmonization: spec competence is version-pinned; repair-session competence evaporates at context boundaries. D2's few-rules cap plus uniformity-as-transfer, F006/SPARK, and the uncited K001 Austral card (learn-the-whole-language-from-the-spec philosophy) make the small fully-taught kernel the best-precedented feasibility story in the corpus.

'One spec line amortizes to zero marginal cost' prices the debate's contested quantity at zero in its own favor — every task re-pays the in-context payload, which is exactly the cost structure D2's motivation clause names. The irregularity-meter (leg 3) cannot discriminate its three causes — D2-irregularity vs prior-interference against a uniform rule vs D1a checker incompleteness rejecting sound patterns — yet routes all persistent error mass to language redesign; that is an uncarded distributional claim at the position's operational core. And the silent-wrong asymmetry, its best leg, actually routes burden to a THIRD channel (by-construction language design + differential/silent-wrong harness, per the record's own decided remedies), not to spec prose; 'can only live in the spec' overreaches, though 'cannot live in the loop' stands.

positionAssessments.position: Curriculum-as-build-artifact (generated, budgeted, CI-gated teaching pack; split discovered by ablation)

Its per-rule ablation experiment (drop rule text, add repair example, measure deltas under fixed budget) is the most precise answer-procedure any position offers for 'how much burden moves', and converting the split from doctrine to a measured knob matches the project's own epistemics (pre-committed thresholds, gate-before-promote). positionAssessments.weaknesses: Systematically overclaims, as the critic documented. 'Only position with a measurement mechanism' is false (P1 ships loop instrumentation, P2 a fleet ledger and spec-ROI curve); its true distinctive — CI-gating language changes on current-model pass rates — is precisely the process-relative acceptance round-3 invariant 4 resists, plus model-idiom lock-in (baking 2026 priors into permanent design). 'The pack is D2's regression test' is a category error: pack tokens and pass rates measure size and model difficulty, not irregularity, D2's stated metric; a uniform verbose rule grows the pack while being owner-ruled spec-cheap. The C004 gloss contradicts Layer 1 (unproven D1-critical facts are runtime-checked under trap=abort — dynamic, one-lesson-per-process-death, not 'complete compile-time lessons'). 'Cannot drift' covers only code subtrees — kernel prose and anti-prior notes can rot, and pack-faithfulness validation is a conceded undesigned deliverable. The closed diagnostics vocabulary cannot close over an open spec (concurrent-trap, bare-'+', interior regime), making 'novel free-text errors are toolchain bugs' vacuous or a permanent blocker.

keyTradeoffs: Eager vs lazy delivery economics: an eager spec pays a fixed per-task context cost for rules most programs never touch; lazy diagnostics pay per-violation full generate/check/re-read iterations (and, for Layer-1 dynamic lessons, one process death each under trap=abort). Unmeasured in both directions; the heavy-tail violation-distribution premise decides the magnitude, and D2's few-rules artifact cap shrinks the delta either way.

Coverage vs economics: the repair loop can only teach checker-visible rules; the classes that matter most under D0 (performance-wrong code, region pessimization, context-dropping, operand swaps) are rejection-invisible by construction, and the adopted soundness-over-completeness lever adds sound-but-rejected restructure demands that are neither spec-rule citations nor mechanical fixes. So allocation can never be purely loop-side regardless of who wins the token economics.

Diagnostic richness vs diagnostic trust: teaching-grade diagnostics (rule text + witness + fix) accelerate repair if the ablation confirms it, but convert diagnostic text into a TCB-adjacent surface where a wrong citation actively teaches a false rule — demanding N006-grade tested-and-versioned maintenance, an unpriced cost carried in proportion to how much teaching the loop does.

Artifact-checkable vs process-relative competence: spec-encoded knowledge is version-pinned, auditable, and identical fleet-wide (extends round-3 invariant 4); repair-session knowledge is per-instance, washes out at context boundaries, and is re-paid per session unless a distillation channel works — and every distillation channel is itself a D2 accretion vector needing the unified gating regime.

Writability gating vs model lock-in: CI-gating language changes on current-model pass rates catches teachability regressions before shipping (J002-style guard) but re-instantiates process-relative acceptance and risks baking today's model priors into permanent language design.

Toolchain proliferation vs charter: the three positions propose three overlapping telemetry systems and three privileged spec-evolution channels over the same checker; shipped separately they reproduce the near-duplicate-machinery drift the charter forbids in source — unification is required, not optional.

result.leadingCandidate: Spec-primary-leaning hybrid on P3's delivery mechanism with P1's diagnostic guarantees as law: a D2-capped kernel spec (few uniform rules + canonical worked examples + the diagnostics/report vocabulary, which must be taught eagerly) generated and versioned as a provenance-checked teaching pack; the repair loop carries only prior-correction, mechanical edit-list propagation, and the conceded nonlocal-ownership residue; every diagnostic is deterministic, one-pass-complete, aggregated, and rule-cited pointing back INTO the spec (reinforcement, not substitute curriculum); checker-invisible quality classes route to by-construction design + differential/silent-wrong harness + lint channels, never to either teaching channel; the exact per-rule split is set by the pre-committed ablation experiment, and P3's writability benchmark runs advisory (not shipping-gate) until the transfer-validity study passes.
result.rationale: The question is genuinely empirical and currently unanswerable to the project's own standard: the corpus has zero AI-codegen cards, the two most-cited teaching anchors (D009, C002) are unverified provisional, and the critic exposed that the debate's core quantity lacks a unit — D2's text (user-directives line 16) binds the language description as an artifact, which already caps the rule count regardless of allocation, but no owner ruling says whether diagnostic-delivered elaborations count against that budget. That definitional gap plus the pending pedagogy experiments force research_needed. Post-critique, however, the positions converge on more than they contest. First, a shared toolchain-guarantee floor survives all attacks and should be adopted now: stable content-addressed rule IDs; deterministic byte-stable diagnostics carrying rule ID, canonical rule text, node-path witness, and edit list where mechanical; complete one-pass error reporting; one-defect-one-repair-site; a rule-to-emitter coverage audit; loop instrumentation; and a single unified telemetry + gated spec-evolution channel (three were proposed; shipped separately they violate the near-duplicate-machinery charter). Second, two structural facts bound the split before any experiment runs: (a) the checker cannot teach what it cannot reject — the D0-critical silent-wrong classes and performance idioms need eager spec/examples plus a differential harness, so the loop can never be primary for what actually matters; (b) the adopted soundness-over-completeness lever creates restructure-demand lessons that are neither spec citations nor mechanical fixes, breaking P1's bijection exactly on the hardest (ownership) errors, while the decided Miri-style dynamic oracle is an unplaced fourth channel. Third, under a D2-compliant few-rules language the eager-vs-lazy token delta shrinks, and the corpus's nearest evidence (K001 Austral spec philosophy, F006 SPARK) favors small-kernel eager feasibility — so the burden of proof sits on moving teaching INTO the loop, and the loop's defensible steady-state role is prior-correction, mechanical propagation, telemetry, and a bounded named ownership residue. P2's direction therefore leads, but stripped of its manufactured legs (the floor-vs-ceiling misreading, the zero-marginal-cost inversion, the three-way-confounded irregularity meter) and mounted on P3's generated-pack mechanism, which is the decided architecture's honest extension for code examples (drift-free) though not for prose. P3's CI gate stays advisory until the benchmark-transfer study passes, because gating language evolution on current-model pass rates is exactly the process-relative acceptance round-3 invariant 4 resists. The named experiments, with the D2 unit ruling first, convert the remaining disagreement into a measured per-rule table.

specArchitecture: SINGLE MACHINE-CHECKED SPEC ARTIFACT (monolith-by-default): the language description — grammar, rules, annotation vocabulary, worked examples — lives in ONE normative, version-pinned, CI-checked artifact. This is the convergent core all three spec-budget positions share once overclaims are stripped: P3's regularity invariants enforced mechanically over the artifact, plus P1's procedural apparatus (counted token AND rule-count deltas on every design change, spec-delta column in decision-gates.md, kernel reserve priced first). 'Hard-in-procedure, number-by-measurement': no numeric token cap has cut authority until the calibration audit and spec-size ablation locate a real degradation knee — pre-registering 10-20k now would engrave an uncarded model-behavior claim (caveats.md standing rule; zero in-context-acquisition cards exist).

KERNEL/LAYERS (P2) IS DEMOTED TO AN EXPERIMENT ARM, NOT ARCHITECTURE: layering grows TOTAL normative mass (extension law, layer registry, provisioning protocol) to shrink resident share, which is rational only if unrun experiments (U1/U7, context-competition, retrieval-substitution) hold; its partition also collides with decided floors (storage/lifetime contracts are mandatory on EVERY allocation per round2-synthesis line 29 — majority-path, not layer material; typed operation tables are core dispatch per line 27). RETAINED from P2 regardless: the conservative-extension law (any future layer only adds vocabulary at kernel extension points; unknown constructs are deterministic hard errors under canonical-form + closed-world), content-addressed version-pinned spec artifacts, and spec provisioning recorded in provenance.

TEACHING PACK AS GENERATED BUILD ARTIFACT (P3's mechanism under spec-primary allocation): the spec + canonical worked examples + the diagnostics/report vocabulary are generated as a deterministic, provenance-checked, content-addressed pack — the round-2 consistency machinery (mechanism 2) applied to pedagogy. Example extraction is drift-free by construction under the single-spelling floor (extracted examples are re-verified subtrees); pack prose can still rot, so pack-faithfulness validation is a named open deliverable. The pack's writability CI gate runs ADVISORY only until the benchmark-transfer validity study passes — gating language evolution on current-model pass rates is the process-relative acceptance round-3 invariant 4 resists.

SPEC-PRIMARY PEDAGOGY, LOOP AS REINFORCEMENT: the compact kernel spec carries the teaching burden; the repair loop carries prior-correction, mechanical edit-list execution, telemetry, and the conceded nonlocal-ownership residue. Every diagnostic is deterministic, byte-stable, one-pass-complete, and rule-cited pointing back INTO the spec (reinforcement, never a substitute lazy curriculum — lazy delivery would remove the cost pressure that enforces the rule cap, and a citation database of 'rule elaborations' would itself be a D2 violation wherever the text lives). Checker-invisible quality classes (region pessimization, context-dropping Result chains, operand swaps, performance-wrong code — D0's actual objective) route to by-construction design + differential/silent-wrong harness + lint channels, never to either teaching channel.

NO-SHADOW-SPEC RULE (perimeter of the counted artifact): ALL normative content the writer must learn in-context counts against the one artifact — explicitly including the machine-readable diagnostics vocabulary, provenance-record schema, site-enumerated edit-list schema, and elaborated re-read view format (round2-synthesis Additions, lines 66-76). These are currently uncounted shadow spec in every proposed regime; corrected D2 makes them first-class budget items. One unified telemetry + gated spec-evolution channel (merging P1 distillation gate / P2 fleet ledger / P3 pack regeneration) — three parallel channels would reproduce the near-duplicate-machinery drift the charter forbids.

SURFACE UNDER THIS ARCHITECTURE (leading candidate from the familiarity ruling): the round-2 alien-regular canonical grammar stands untouched (uniform node shape; Rust-shaped node inventory stays out — it adds productions, exactly what corrected D2 charges); lexicon may be selectively borrowed from the Rust prior only after a divergence census, with fully self-contained definitions (familiar names as mnemonics, no definition-by-delta, minted tokens at every divergence point such as unchecked-UB vs nsw/nuw-poison). Lexicon is first-order spec-budget-neutral; grammar shape is not. The parked bijective write-codec keeps familiarity purchasable later as tooling at zero spec cost — the reversibility asymmetry that de-risks keeping the canonical core alien.
